Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```csharp\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n//  FILE:    src/module_12.txt   (C# source code file)\n//\n//  NOTE:    Extension \u201c.txt\u201d is intentional\u2014files are stitched together by an\n//           internal build-tool that renames/compiles them.  Treat as .cs.\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n//\n//  Description\n//  ===========\n//  TransformationCoordinator is part of PulseOps Warehouse\u2019s pluggable\n//  transformation layer.  It wires together the Strategy-Pattern implementation\n//  so that new data-sources can be onboarded by simply dropping a new\n//  IDataTransformationStrategy implementation into the DI container.\n//\n//  The coordinator fan-outs events coming from a Kinesis or SQS trigger, routes\n//  them to the correct transformation strategy, records structured metrics, and\n//  handles faults with circuit-breaker semantics.\n//\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Collections.Concurrent;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Diagnostics;\nusing System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;\nusing System.Text.Json;\nusing System.Threading;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\n\nnamespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Transformations\n{\n    #region Public Contracts \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     A canonical event representation flowing through the transformation\n    ///     pipeline.  Keeps only the information required for choosing and\n    ///     performing a transformation.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed record StreamEvent(\n        string StreamType,\n        JsonElement Payload,\n        DateTimeOffset Timestamp\n    );\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Transformations convert raw <see cref=\"StreamEvent\"/> instances into\n    ///     domain-level warehouse records (e.g., Parquet rows, Fact tables).\n    /// </summary>\n    /// <typeparam name=\"TOut\">Final representation (e.g., POCO, Avro object).</typeparam>\n    public interface IDataTransformationStrategy<out TOut>\n    {\n        /// <summary>\n        ///     Performs asynchronous transformation with cancellation support.\n        /// </summary>\n        ValueTask<TOut> TransformAsync(StreamEvent @event, CancellationToken token);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Allows lookup of strategies by stream identifier.\n    /// </summary>\n    public interface IStrategyRegistry\n    {\n        bool TryResolve<TOut>(\n            string streamType,\n            [NotNullWhen(true)] out IDataTransformationStrategy<TOut>? strategy);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Minimal contract for pushing custom metrics into CloudWatch/Prometheus.\n    /// </summary>\n    public interface IMetricsRecorder\n    {\n        void Increment(string metricName, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? dimensions = null);\n        void Timing(string metricName, TimeSpan elapsed, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? dimensions = null);\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Exceptions \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Indicates a transformation failure that should be routed to DLQ.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class DataTransformationException : Exception\n    {\n        public DataTransformationException(string message, Exception? inner) : base(message, inner) { }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Thrown when an unknown stream type is encountered.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class StrategyNotFoundException : Exception\n    {\n        public StrategyNotFoundException(string streamType)\n            : base($\"No transformation strategy registered for streamType = '{streamType}'\") { }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Coordinator \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Central router that fan-outs incoming events to the appropriate\n    ///     <see cref=\"IDataTransformationStrategy{TOut}\" />.  Handles parallel\n    ///     execution, observability, retries, and error surfacing.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class TransformationCoordinator\n    {\n        private readonly IStrategyRegistry _registry;\n        private readonly ILogger<TransformationCoordinator> _logger;\n        private readonly IMetricsRecorder _metrics;\n        private readonly int _maxConcurrency;\n\n        public TransformationCoordinator(\n            IStrategyRegistry registry,\n            ILogger<TransformationCoordinator> logger,\n            IMetricsRecorder metrics,\n            int maxConcurrency = 8)\n        {\n            _registry = registry ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(registry));\n            _logger   = logger   ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(logger));\n            _metrics  = metrics  ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(metrics));\n            _maxConcurrency = Math.Clamp(maxConcurrency, 1, Environment.ProcessorCount * 2);\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        ///     Processes a batch of <paramref name=\"events\"/> concurrently while\n        ///     respecting <see cref=\"_maxConcurrency\"/>.\n        /// </summary>\n        public async Task HandleBatchAsync(\n            IReadOnlyCollection<StreamEvent> events,\n            CancellationToken token)\n        {\n            if (events is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(events));\n            if (events.Count == 0) return;\n\n            _logger.LogDebug(\"Starting batch transformation for {Count} events.\", events.Count);\n\n            // Use a concurrent bag to capture failures for consolidated reporting\n            var failures = new ConcurrentBag<Exception>();\n\n            await Parallel.ForEachAsync(\n                events,\n                new ParallelOptions\n                {\n                    MaxDegreeOfParallelism = _maxConcurrency,\n                    CancellationToken      = token\n                },\n                async (@event, ct) =>\n                {\n                    try\n                    {\n                        await HandleSingleAsync(@event, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n                    }\n                    catch (Exception ex)\n                    {\n                        failures.Add(ex);\n                    }\n                }).ConfigureAwait(false);\n\n            if (!failures.IsEmpty)\n            {\n                _logger.LogError(\"{Count} events failed during transformation.\", failures.Count);\n                throw new AggregateException(\"One or more events failed transformation.\", failures);\n            }\n\n            _logger.LogInformation(\"Batch transformation completed successfully.\");\n        }\n\n        private async Task HandleSingleAsync(StreamEvent @event, CancellationToken ct)\n        {\n            var stopwatch = Stopwatch.StartNew();\n\n            if (!_registry.TryResolve<object?>(@event.StreamType, out var strategy))\n            {\n                _metrics.Increment(\"Transformation.UnknownStreamType\", new Dictionary<string, string>\n                {\n                    [\"StreamType\"] = @event.StreamType\n                });\n\n                _logger.LogWarning(\n                    \"Skipping event with unknown StreamType: {StreamType}.\",\n                    @event.StreamType);\n\n                throw new StrategyNotFoundException(@event.StreamType);\n            }\n\n            try\n            {\n                var output = await strategy!.TransformAsync(@event, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n\n                // Downstream dispatch placeholder (e.g., to Firehose/Glue)\n                _logger.LogTrace(\n                    \"Transformed event from {StreamType} into {OutputType}.\",\n                    @event.StreamType, output?.GetType().Name ?? \"<null>\");\n\n                _metrics.Increment(\"Transformation.Success\", new Dictionary<string, string>\n                {\n                    [\"StreamType\"] = @event.StreamType\n                });\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n                _logger.LogError(ex,\n                    \"Transformation failed for StreamType = {StreamType}.\",\n                    @event.StreamType);\n\n                _metrics.Increment(\"Transformation.Failure\", new Dictionary<string, string>\n                {\n                    [\"StreamType\"] = @event.StreamType\n                });\n\n                throw new DataTransformationException(\n                    $\"Transformation failed for StreamType = {@event.StreamType}\", ex);\n            }\n            finally\n            {\n                stopwatch.Stop();\n                _metrics.Timing(\"Transformation.Latency\", stopwatch.Elapsed,\n                    new Dictionary<string, string> { [\"StreamType\"] = @event.StreamType });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Registry Implementation \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Thread-safe registry that supports hot-swapping strategies via DI.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class StrategyRegistry : IStrategyRegistry\n    {\n        private readonly IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> _map;\n\n        /// <summary>\n        ///     Instantiates the registry with an immutable map of streamType \u2192 strategy.\n        /// </summary>\n        public StrategyRegistry(IEnumerable<(string StreamType, object Strategy)> registrations)\n        {\n            if (registrations is null)\n                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(registrations));\n\n            var map = new Dictionary<string, object>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n            foreach (var (streamType, strategy) in registrations)\n            {\n                ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(streamType);\n                ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(strategy);\n\n                map[streamType] = strategy;\n            }\n\n            _map = map;\n        }\n\n        public bool TryResolve<TOut>(\n            string streamType,\n            [NotNullWhen(true)] out IDataTransformationStrategy<TOut>? strategy)\n        {\n            strategy = null;\n\n            if (_map.TryGetValue(streamType, out var obj) &&\n                obj is IDataTransformationStrategy<TOut> typed)\n            {\n                strategy = typed;\n                return true;\n            }\n\n            return false;\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Example Strategies \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Sample transformation for stream \u201ccalendar.events\u201d.  Converts raw JSON\n    ///     into a strongly-typed <see cref=\"CalendarFact\"/> record.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class CalendarEventTransformationStrategy\n        : IDataTransformationStrategy<CalendarFact>\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger<CalendarEventTransformationStrategy> _logger;\n\n        public CalendarEventTransformationStrategy(\n            ILogger<CalendarEventTransformationStrategy> logger)\n        {\n            _logger = logger;\n        }\n\n        public ValueTask<CalendarFact> TransformAsync(\n            StreamEvent @event,\n            CancellationToken token)\n        {\n            // Artificial processing delay for demonstration\n            token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();\n\n            CalendarRaw raw = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<CalendarRaw>(\n                @event.Payload.GetRawText())!;\n\n            var fact = new CalendarFact(\n                Id: raw.Id,\n                Organizer: raw.Organizer,\n                StartUtc: raw.StartUtc,\n                EndUtc: raw.EndUtc,\n                AttendeeCount: raw.Attendees.Length,\n                DurationMinutes: (int)(raw.EndUtc - raw.StartUtc).TotalMinutes,\n                ExtractedOnUtc: DateTimeOffset.UtcNow);\n\n            _logger.LogDebug(\"Computed CalendarFact for event {Id}\", fact.Id);\n\n            return ValueTask.FromResult(fact);\n        }\n\n        // DTOs for demonstration only\n        private sealed record CalendarRaw(\n            string Id,\n            string Organizer,\n            DateTimeOffset StartUtc,\n            DateTimeOffset EndUtc,\n            string[] Attendees);\n\n        public sealed record CalendarFact(\n            string Id,\n            string Organizer,\n            DateTimeOffset StartUtc,\n            DateTimeOffset EndUtc,\n            int AttendeeCount,\n            int DurationMinutes,\n            DateTimeOffset ExtractedOnUtc);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    ///     Demonstrates transformation of CI/CD log events into deployment facts.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class CiLogTransformationStrategy : IDataTransformationStrategy<DeploymentFact>\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger<CiLogTransformationStrategy> _logger;\n\n        public CiLogTransformationStrategy(\n            ILogger<CiLogTransformationStrategy> logger)\n        {\n            _logger = logger;\n        }\n\n        public async ValueTask<DeploymentFact> TransformAsync(\n            StreamEvent @event,\n            CancellationToken token)\n        {\n            token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();\n            // Simulate I/O workload\u2014e.g., fetch commit metadata from GitHub API\n            await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(50), token).ConfigureAwait(false);\n\n            CiLogRaw raw = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<CiLogRaw>(\n                @event.Payload.GetRawText())!;\n\n            var fact = new DeploymentFact(\n                PipelineId: raw.PipelineId,\n                Repo: raw.Repo,\n                CommitSha: raw.CommitSha,\n                Status: raw.Status,\n                LeadTimeSeconds: raw.LeadTimeSec,\n                ProducedOnUtc: DateTimeOffset.UtcNow);\n\n            _logger.LogInformation(\n                \"Produced DeploymentFact for pipeline {PipelineId}\", fact.PipelineId);\n\n            return fact;\n        }\n\n        private sealed record CiLogRaw(\n            string PipelineId,\n            string Repo,\n            string CommitSha,\n            string Status,\n            int LeadTimeSec);\n\n        public sealed record DeploymentFact(\n            string PipelineId,\n            string Repo,\n            string CommitSha,\n            string Status,\n            int LeadTimeSeconds,\n            DateTimeOffset ProducedOnUtc);\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_12.txt",
    "numLines": 392,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 392
  },
  "type": "text"
}

47. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_8.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```csharp\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n//  Project : PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n//  File    : src/module_8.txt         (logical source file: Module_8.cs)\n//  Author  : PulseOps Engineering Team\n//  Purpose : Implements the dynamic Transformation-Strategy runtime that powers\n//            pluggable KPI calculations inside the analytics fabric.\n//            Exposes:\n//              \u2022 ITransformationStrategy  \u2013 canonical strategy contract\n//              \u2022 TransformationStrategyAttribute \u2013 declarative registration\n//              \u2022 StrategyRegistry         \u2013 reflection-based discovery/DI glue\n//              \u2022 TransformationExecutor   \u2013 concurrent orchestration & telemetry\n//              \u2022 Example concrete strategy (FocusTimeRatioStrategy)\n//\n//  Note    : Although this file has a .txt path suffix in the repository, the\n//            build pipeline aliases it to a .cs compilation unit. This trick\n//            enables litterate-spec documentation tooling downstream.\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Collections.Concurrent;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Diagnostics;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing System.Reflection;\nusing System.Text.Json;\nusing System.Threading;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Amazon.CloudWatch;\nusing Amazon.CloudWatch.Model;\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions;\nusing PulseOps.Warehouse.Shared;\n\n// ReSharper disable MemberCanBePrivate.Global\n// ReSharper disable UnusedType.Global\n\nnamespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Transformations\n{\n    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Contracts \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Canonical contract that every KPI transformation strategy must implement.\n    /// </summary>\n    /// <typeparam name=\"TIn\">Normalized input record.</typeparam>\n    /// <typeparam name=\"TOut\">Enriched / calculated record.</typeparam>\n    public interface ITransformationStrategy<in TIn, TOut>\n    {\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Executes the strategy asynchronously.\n        /// </summary>\n        /// <param name=\"input\">Stream (enumerable) of incoming records.</param>\n        /// <param name=\"context\">Request-scoped metadata, DI container, etc.</param>\n        /// <param name=\"cancellationToken\">Co-operative cancellation token.</param>\n        /// <returns>Transformed records.</returns>\n        IAsyncEnumerable<TOut> TransformAsync(\n            IAsyncEnumerable<TIn> input,\n            TransformationContext context,\n            CancellationToken cancellationToken = default);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Attribute used to declaratively register a strategy implementation.\n    /// </summary>\n    [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class, AllowMultiple = false)]\n    public sealed class TransformationStrategyAttribute : Attribute\n    {\n        public TransformationStrategyAttribute(string name, string version = \"1.0\")\n        {\n            Name    = name ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(name));\n            Version = version;\n        }\n\n        public string Name    { get; }\n        public string Version { get; }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Bag-object holding request-scoped services, metadata and DI.\n    /// Instances are short-lived and safe for concurrent use.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class TransformationContext\n    {\n        public TransformationContext(\n            Guid requestId,\n            string tenantId,\n            ILogger? logger           = null,\n            IAmazonCloudWatch? cw     = null,\n            IDictionary<string, object>? items = null)\n        {\n            RequestId = requestId;\n            TenantId  = tenantId;\n            Logger    = logger ?? NullLogger.Instance;\n            CloudWatchClient = cw;\n            Items     = new ConcurrentDictionary<string, object>(\n                items ?? new Dictionary<string, object>());\n        }\n\n        public Guid RequestId { get; }\n        public string TenantId { get; }\n        public ILogger Logger { get; }\n        public IAmazonCloudWatch? CloudWatchClient { get; }\n        public ConcurrentDictionary<string, object> Items { get; }\n\n        public T GetOrAdd<T>(string key, Func<string, T> factory) =>\n            (T) Items.GetOrAdd(key, k => factory(k));\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Registry & Discovery \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Discovers and caches all <see cref=\"ITransformationStrategy{TIn,TOut}\"/>\n    /// implementations at runtime using reflection. Thread-safe singleton.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class StrategyRegistry\n    {\n        private static readonly Lazy<StrategyRegistry> _lazy =\n            new(() => new StrategyRegistry());\n\n        public static StrategyRegistry Instance => _lazy.Value;\n\n        private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, Type> _strategies = new();\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n\n        private StrategyRegistry(ILogger? logger = null)\n        {\n            _logger = logger ?? NullLogger.Instance;\n\n            // Scan the current AppDomain once. Subsequent loads (Lambda cold-start)\n            // are negligible.\n            foreach (Assembly assembly in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())\n            {\n                try\n                {\n                    LoadStrategiesFromAssembly(assembly);\n                }\n                catch (ReflectionTypeLoadException e)\n                {\n                    _logger.LogWarning(\n                        e,\n                        \"Failed to reflectively load strategy types from assembly {Assembly}. \" +\n                        \"Loader exceptions: {LoaderExceptions}\",\n                        assembly.FullName,\n                        string.Join(\"; \",\n                            e.LoaderExceptions.Select(le => le.Message)));\n                }\n            }\n\n            _logger.LogInformation(\n                \"StrategyRegistry initialized with {Count} strategies.\",\n                _strategies.Count);\n        }\n\n        public bool TryResolve(string name, out Type strategyType) =>\n            _strategies.TryGetValue(name, out strategyType);\n\n        private void LoadStrategiesFromAssembly(Assembly assembly)\n        {\n            foreach (Type type in assembly.GetTypes())\n            {\n                if (type.IsAbstract || type.IsInterface) continue;\n\n                var strategyAttr = type.GetCustomAttribute<TransformationStrategyAttribute>();\n                if (strategyAttr == null) continue;\n\n                // Only consider generic type definitions ITransformationStrategy<,>.\n                bool implementsInterface = type\n                    .GetInterfaces()\n                    .Any(i =>\n                        i.IsGenericType &&\n                        i.GetGenericTypeDefinition() == typeof(ITransformationStrategy<,>));\n\n                if (!implementsInterface)\n                {\n                    _logger.LogWarning(\n                        \"Type {Type} is decorated with {Attribute} but does not implement \" +\n                        \"the required ITransformationStrategy<,> contract. Skipped.\",\n                        type.FullName,\n                        nameof(TransformationStrategyAttribute));\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                string key = BuildKey(strategyAttr.Name, strategyAttr.Version);\n                if (!_strategies.TryAdd(key, type))\n                {\n                    _logger.LogWarning(\n                        \"Duplicate strategy registration detected for key '{Key}'. \" +\n                        \"Type {Type} ignored (existing: {Existing}).\",\n                        key,\n                        type.FullName,\n                        _strategies[key].FullName);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private static string BuildKey(string name, string version) => $\"{name}:{version}\";\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Executor / Orchestration \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Convenience wrapper around <see cref=\"StrategyRegistry\"/> that handles:\n    ///   \u2022 DI-friendly construction\n    ///   \u2022 Concurrency throttling (fan-out)\n    ///   \u2022 Structured logging & CloudWatch custom metrics\n    ///   \u2022 Graceful error propagation (fault-tolerant)\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class TransformationExecutor\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n        private readonly int _maxDegreeOfParallelism;\n        private readonly IServiceProvider? _serviceProvider;\n\n        public TransformationExecutor(\n            ILogger? logger = null,\n            int maxDegreeOfParallelism = 4,\n            IServiceProvider? serviceProvider = null)\n        {\n            _logger = logger ?? NullLogger.Instance;\n            _maxDegreeOfParallelism = Math.Max(1, maxDegreeOfParallelism);\n            _serviceProvider = serviceProvider;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// High-level entrypoint used by Lambda handlers and Step-Function tasks.\n        /// </summary>\n        public async Task<IReadOnlyList<object>> ExecuteAsync(\n            string strategyName,\n            string strategyVersion,\n            IAsyncEnumerable<object> stream,\n            TransformationContext context,\n            CancellationToken token = default)\n        {\n            var sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();\n            string key = $\"{strategyName}:{strategyVersion}\";\n\n            if (!StrategyRegistry.Instance.TryResolve(key, out Type strategyType))\n            {\n                throw new InvalidOperationException(\n                    $\"Strategy '{key}' is not registered. \" +\n                    \"Ensure the assembly containing the strategy is loaded.\");\n            }\n\n            // Build the strategy instance via DI or reflection fallback.\n            object? instance = _serviceProvider?.GetService(strategyType)\n                               ?? Activator.CreateInstance(strategyType);\n            if (instance is null)\n                throw new InvalidOperationException(\n                    $\"Failed to instantiate strategy '{key}'. \" +\n                    \"Make sure it has a public parameterless constructor or is registered in DI.\");\n\n            // Generic variance gymnastics: use reflection to call TransformAsync and materialize.\n            MethodInfo? transformMethod = strategyType\n                .GetMethod(nameof(ITransformationStrategy<object, object>.TransformAsync));\n            if (transformMethod is null)\n                throw new MissingMethodException(strategyType.FullName,\n                    nameof(ITransformationStrategy<object, object>.TransformAsync));\n\n            _logger.LogInformation(\n                \"[{RequestId}] Executing strategy {Strategy} for tenant {Tenant}.\",\n                context.RequestId,\n                key,\n                context.TenantId);\n\n            CancellationTokenSource linkedCts = CancellationTokenSource\n                .CreateLinkedTokenSource(token);\n\n            try\n            {\n                var resultStream = (IAsyncEnumerable<object>) transformMethod\n                    .Invoke(instance, new object[] { stream, context, linkedCts.Token })!;\n\n                // Materialize results in parallel to enable fan-out.\n                List<object> results = new();\n                SemaphoreSlim gate = new(_maxDegreeOfParallelism);\n\n                await foreach (object item in resultStream\n                                   .WithCancellation(linkedCts.Token))\n                {\n                    await gate.WaitAsync(linkedCts.Token);\n                    _ = Task.Run(() =>\n                        {\n                            try\n                            {\n                                lock (results)\n                                {\n                                    results.Add(item);\n                                }\n                            }\n                            finally\n                            {\n                                gate.Release();\n                            }\n                        }, linkedCts.Token);\n                }\n\n                // Wait for outstanding tasks to complete.\n                for (int i = 0; i < _maxDegreeOfParallelism; i++)\n                    await gate.WaitAsync(linkedCts.Token);\n\n                sw.Stop();\n                _logger.LogInformation(\n                    \"[{RequestId}] Strategy {Strategy} produced {RecordCount} records in {ElapsedMs} ms.\",\n                    context.RequestId,\n                    key,\n                    results.Count,\n                    sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);\n\n                await PublishMetricAsync(\n                    metricName: \"StrategyLatencyMs\",\n                    value: sw.ElapsedMilliseconds,\n                    dimensions: new Dictionary<string, string>\n                    {\n                        [\"Strategy\"] = strategyName,\n                        [\"Tenant\"]   = context.TenantId\n                    },\n                    context.CloudWatchClient,\n                    linkedCts.Token);\n\n                return results;\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex) when (!linkedCts.IsCancellationRequested)\n            {\n                _logger.LogError(\n                    ex,\n                    \"[{RequestId}] Strategy {Strategy} failed: {Message}\",\n                    context.RequestId,\n                    key,\n                    ex.Message);\n                throw;\n            }\n            finally\n            {\n                linkedCts.Dispose();\n            }\n        }\n\n        private static async Task PublishMetricAsync(\n            string metricName,\n            double value,\n            IDictionary<string, string> dimensions,\n            IAmazonCloudWatch? client,\n            CancellationToken token)\n        {\n            if (client == null) return;\n\n            var metricDatum = new MetricDatum\n            {\n                MetricName = metricName,\n                Timestamp  = DateTime.UtcNow,\n                Unit       = StandardUnit.Milliseconds,\n                Value      = value,\n                Dimensions = dimensions.Select(\n                    kv => new Dimension {Name = kv.Key, Value = kv.Value}).ToList()\n            };\n\n            await client.PutMetricDataAsync(new PutMetricDataRequest\n            {\n                Namespace   = \"PulseOps/Warehouse\",\n                MetricData  = new List<MetricDatum> { metricDatum }\n            }, token);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Sample Domain Models \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n\n    // NOTE: In the actual repository these live in a shared project. Included\n    // here for self-containment of this code example.\n\n    public sealed record CalendarEvent(\n        DateTime StartUtc,\n        DateTime EndUtc,\n        bool     IsFocusTime);\n\n    public sealed record FocusTimeRatioKpi(\n        DateTime WindowStartUtc,\n        DateTime WindowEndUtc,\n        double   Ratio);\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Example Concrete Strategy \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Calculates the \"Focus-Time Ratio\" KPI for a sliding window.\n    ///\n    /// Business logic:\n    ///   ratio = total_focus_time / total_calendar_time (within window)\n    ///   \u2022 Focus slots are identified via input.IsFocusTime\n    ///   \u2022 Empty windows yield ratio = 0\n    /// </summary>\n    [TransformationStrategy(\"FocusTimeRatio\", \"2.1\")]\n    public sealed class FocusTimeRatioStrategy\n        : ITransformationStrategy<CalendarEvent, FocusTimeRatioKpi>\n    {\n        public async IAsyncEnumerable<FocusTimeRatioKpi> TransformAsync(\n            IAsyncEnumerable<CalendarEvent> input,\n            TransformationContext context,\n            [System.Runtime.CompilerServices.EnumeratorCancellation]\n            CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n        {\n            var window = new List<CalendarEvent>();\n            TimeSpan windowLength = TimeSpan.FromHours(24); // daily window\n            DateTime? currentWindowStart = null;\n\n            await foreach (CalendarEvent evt in input\n                               .WithCancellation(cancellationToken))\n            {\n                if (currentWindowStart == null)\n                    currentWindowStart = FloorToDay(evt.StartUtc);\n\n                DateTime windowEnd = currentWindowStart.Value + windowLength;\n                if (evt.StartUtc >= windowEnd)\n                {\n                    // Emit KPI for completed window.\n                    yield return Compute(window, currentWindowStart.Value, windowEnd);\n\n                    // Advance window boundary. Assuming chronological order.\n                    currentWindowStart = FloorToDay(evt.StartUtc);\n                    window.Clear();\n                }\n\n                window.Add(evt);\n            }\n\n            // Flush the last window.\n            if (window.Count > 0 && currentWindowStart != null)\n            {\n                DateTime windowEnd = currentWindowStart.Value + windowLength;\n                yield return Compute(window, currentWindowStart.Value, windowEnd);\n            }\n\n            static DateTime FloorToDay(DateTime dt) => dt.Date;\n\n            static FocusTimeRatioKpi Compute(\n                IReadOnlyCollection<CalendarEvent> events,\n                DateTime from,\n                DateTime to)\n            {\n                double focusMinutes = events\n                    .Where(e => e.IsFocusTime)\n                    .Sum(e => (e.EndUtc - e.StartUtc).TotalMinutes);\n\n                double totalMinutes = events\n                    .Sum(e => (e.EndUtc - e.StartUtc).TotalMinutes);\n\n                double ratio = totalMinutes == 0 ? 0 : focusMinutes / totalMinutes;\n\n                return new FocusTimeRatioKpi(from, to, ratio);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Extension-Helpers \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n\n    internal static class AsyncEnumerableExtensions\n    {\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Serializes an async-enumerable to NDJSON for debugging/tracing purposes.\n        /// </summary>\n        public static async Task<string> ToJsonLinesAsync<T>(\n            this IAsyncEnumerable<T> source,\n            CancellationToken token = default)\n        {\n            var jsonOptions = new JsonSerializerOptions\n            {\n                WriteIndented = false\n            };\n\n            List<string> lines = new();\n            await foreach (T item in source.WithCancellation(token))\n            {\n                string json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(item, jsonOptions);\n                lines.Add(json);\n            }\n\n            return string.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_8.txt",
    "numLines": 491,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 491
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "```csharp\n// File: src/module_75.txt\n// Project: PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n// Language: C# 10\n// ---------------------------------------------\n// This module implements a reusable, back-pressure-aware time-series\n// aggregation engine that can be plugged into any Lambda/Kinesis\n// micro-component.  It embraces the Observer pattern in order to be\n// embeddable in both streaming (push) and batch (pull) scenarios while\n// still preserving functional purity through a user-supplied\n// aggregation delegate.\n//\n// Key capabilities\n// \u2022 Tumbling & sliding windows                                             \n// \u2022 Thread-safe, lock-free hot path                                        \n// \u2022 Back-pressure aware via System.Threading.Channels                      \n// \u2022 Real-time Observable<KpiMetric> output                                 \n// \u2022 Fault-tolerant (bubble-up strategy / DLQ compatible)                   \n// ---------------------------------------------\n#nullable enable\nusing System;\nusing System.Collections.Concurrent;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Globalization;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing System.Reactive.Linq;\nusing System.Reactive.Subjects;\nusing System.Runtime.CompilerServices;\nusing System.Text.Json;\nusing System.Threading;\nusing System.Threading.Channels;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\n\nnamespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Transformations\n{\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Single domain event coming from any ingestion source.\n    /// A deliberately flat structure that can be re-shaped downstream.\n    /// </summary>\n    /// <param name=\"Timestamp\">UTC timestamp the event occurred.</param>\n    /// <param name=\"EventType\">Human-friendly event discriminator.</param>\n    /// <param name=\"ActorId\">Primary user / system identifier.</param>\n    /// <param name=\"NumericValue\">\n    /// Optional raw measurement (e.g. duration seconds, bytes, \u2026).\n    /// When null the <see cref=\"Attributes\"/> collection must carry meaning.\n    /// </param>\n    /// <param name=\"Attributes\">Catch-all property bag.</param>\n    public sealed record ProductivityEvent(\n        DateTimeOffset Timestamp,\n        string EventType,\n        string ActorId,\n        decimal? NumericValue,\n        IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? Attributes);\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Output of the aggregator \u2013 one atomic KPI.\n    /// </summary>\n    /// <param name=\"WindowStart\">Left-inclusive boundary.</param>\n    /// <param name=\"WindowEnd\">Right-exclusive boundary.</param>\n    /// <param name=\"MetricName\">Business-level name (e.g. \"FocusTimeRatio\").</param>\n    /// <param name=\"Value\">Aggregated numeric value.</param>\n    /// <param name=\"DimensionKeys\">Partition keys used for slicing/dicing.</param>\n    public sealed record KpiMetric(\n        DateTimeOffset WindowStart,\n        DateTimeOffset WindowEnd,\n        string MetricName,\n        decimal Value,\n        IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? DimensionKeys);\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Sliding or tumbling time window policy.\n    /// </summary>\n    public enum TimeWindowType\n    {\n        Tumbling,\n        Sliding\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Configuration object for <see cref=\"TimeSeriesWindowAggregator\"/>.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class TimeWindowOptions\n    {\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Total width of the window.  Must be \u2265 <see cref=\"SlideInterval\"/>.\n        /// </summary>\n        public required TimeSpan WindowSize { get; init; }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Distance between consecutive window emit points.\n        /// For tumbling windows this acts as window size as well.\n        /// </summary>\n        public required TimeSpan SlideInterval { get; init; }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Whether watermarking (late event allowance) is active.\n        /// </summary>\n        public TimeSpan AllowedLateness { get; init; } = TimeSpan.Zero;\n\n        public TimeWindowType WindowType { get; init; } = TimeWindowType.Tumbling;\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Safety check to avoid mis-configuration at runtime.\n        /// </summary>\n        /// <exception cref=\"ArgumentException\"></exception>\n        public void Validate()\n        {\n            if (WindowSize <= TimeSpan.Zero)\n                throw new ArgumentException($\"{nameof(WindowSize)} must be > 0\", nameof(WindowSize));\n\n            if (SlideInterval <= TimeSpan.Zero)\n                throw new ArgumentException($\"{nameof(SlideInterval)} must be > 0\", nameof(SlideInterval));\n\n            if (SlideInterval > WindowSize && WindowType == TimeWindowType.Sliding)\n                throw new ArgumentException(\n                    $\"{nameof(SlideInterval)} cannot exceed {nameof(WindowSize)} for sliding windows.\", nameof(SlideInterval));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Calculates a KPI value from a set of <see cref=\"ProductivityEvent\"/> items.\n    /// </summary>\n    /// <param name=\"windowEvents\">Events belonging to one complete time window.</param>\n    /// <returns>The calculated KPI metric(s).</returns>\n    public delegate IEnumerable<KpiMetric> KpiAggregationDelegate(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityEvent> windowEvents,\n        DateTimeOffset windowStart,\n        DateTimeOffset windowEnd);\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Reactive, high-performance aggregation engine.\n    /// Implements IObserver for ingestion and IObservable for metric fan-out.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class TimeSeriesWindowAggregator : IObserver<ProductivityEvent>, IObservable<KpiMetric>, IAsyncDisposable\n    {\n        private readonly KpiAggregationDelegate _aggregationLogic;\n        private readonly TimeWindowOptions _options;\n\n        private readonly Subject<KpiMetric> _metricStream = new();\n        private readonly Channel<ProductivityEvent> _inputChannel;\n\n        // Concurrency primitives\n        private readonly CancellationTokenSource _cts = new();\n        private readonly Task _backgroundTask;\n\n        // Internal buffer storing events inside the current active window(s)\n        // We deliberately use a ConcurrentQueue to keep insertion O(1) and avoid\n        // global locks on the hot path.\n        private readonly ConcurrentQueue<ProductivityEvent> _buffer = new();\n\n        // Latest watermark processed (monotonic)\n        private DateTimeOffset _currentWindowCutoff;\n\n        public TimeSeriesWindowAggregator(\n            KpiAggregationDelegate aggregationLogic,\n            TimeWindowOptions options,\n            int boundedCapacity = 10_000) // backpressure\n        {\n            options.Validate();\n\n            _aggregationLogic = aggregationLogic ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(aggregationLogic));\n            _options = options;\n\n            _inputChannel = Channel.CreateBounded<ProductivityEvent>(new BoundedChannelOptions(boundedCapacity)\n            {\n                SingleReader = true,\n                SingleWriter = false,\n                FullMode = BoundedChannelFullMode.Wait\n            });\n\n            _currentWindowCutoff = AlignToWindowStart(DateTimeOffset.UtcNow);\n\n            _backgroundTask = Task.Factory.StartNew(\n                WorkLoopAsync,\n                _cts.Token,\n                TaskCreationOptions.LongRunning,\n                TaskScheduler.Default).Unwrap();\n        }\n\n        #region IObserver\n\n        public void OnNext(ProductivityEvent value)\n        {\n            if (!_inputChannel.Writer.TryWrite(value))\n            {\n                // backpressure fallback \u2013 should never happen due to boundedChannel Wait\n                throw new InvalidOperationException(\"Input channel rejected write unexpectedly.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        public void OnError(Exception error)\n        {\n            // Bubble up to downstream subscribers \u2013 they'll decide what to do.\n            _metricStream.OnError(error);\n        }\n\n        public void OnCompleted()\n        {\n            _inputChannel.Writer.Complete();\n        }\n\n        #endregion\n\n        #region IObservable\n\n        public IDisposable Subscribe(IObserver<KpiMetric> observer)\n        {\n            return _metricStream.AsObservable().Subscribe(observer);\n        }\n\n        #endregion\n\n        private async Task WorkLoopAsync()\n        {\n            try\n            {\n                var reader = _inputChannel.Reader;\n\n                while (await reader.WaitToReadAsync(_cts.Token).ConfigureAwait(false))\n                {\n                    while (reader.TryRead(out var evt))\n                    {\n                        _buffer.Enqueue(evt);\n                    }\n\n                    FlushExpiredWindows();\n                }\n            }\n            catch (OperationCanceledException)\n            {\n                // Normal shutdown \u2013 ignore.\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n                // Surface unexpected exceptions so ops team is alerted (PagerDuty).\n                _metricStream.OnError(ex);\n            }\n            finally\n            {\n                _metricStream.OnCompleted();\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Remove records that no longer belong to any open window and emit\n        /// completed windows downstream.\n        /// </summary>\n        [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]\n        private void FlushExpiredWindows()\n        {\n            var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;\n\n            // Move watermark forward in increments of SlideInterval until the current time is covered.\n            while (_currentWindowCutoff + _options.WindowSize <= now)\n            {\n                var windowStart = _currentWindowCutoff;\n                var windowEnd = windowStart + _options.WindowSize;\n\n                // Gather relevant events.\n                var windowEvents = DrainEvents(windowStart, windowEnd);\n\n                // Apply user-supplied aggregation delegate.\n                try\n                {\n                    foreach (var metric in _aggregationLogic(windowEvents, windowStart, windowEnd))\n                    {\n                        _metricStream.OnNext(metric);\n                    }\n                }\n                catch (Exception ex)\n                {\n                    // Fail-fast on faulty business logic so we don't silently produce wrong numbers.\n                    _metricStream.OnError(new AggregationException(\n                        $\"Aggregation delegate threw for window {windowStart:o}-{windowEnd:o}\", ex));\n                }\n\n                // Advance watermark\n                _currentWindowCutoff = windowStart + _options.SlideInterval;\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Retrieve and remove events from the buffer that are part of the\n        /// requested window interval.\n        /// </summary>\n        private IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityEvent> DrainEvents(DateTimeOffset windowStart, DateTimeOffset windowEnd)\n        {\n            var set = new List<ProductivityEvent>(capacity: 1024);\n\n            while (_buffer.TryPeek(out var candidate))\n            {\n                if (candidate.Timestamp < windowStart)\n                {\n                    // Too old: discard\n                    _buffer.TryDequeue(out _);\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                if (candidate.Timestamp >= windowEnd)\n                {\n                    // Still in future window \u2013 stop draining.\n                    break;\n                }\n\n                // In window range\n                _buffer.TryDequeue(out var inWindow);\n                if (inWindow != null)\n                {\n                    set.Add(inWindow);\n                }\n            }\n\n            return set;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Aligns the given timestamp to the previous aligned window boundary.\n        /// </summary>\n        private DateTimeOffset AlignToWindowStart(DateTimeOffset timestamp)\n        {\n            var ticks = (long)(_options.SlideInterval.Ticks *\n                               Math.Floor(timestamp.UtcTicks / (double)_options.SlideInterval.Ticks));\n            return new DateTimeOffset(ticks, TimeSpan.Zero);\n        }\n\n        public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()\n        {\n            _cts.Cancel();\n\n            // Wait for graceful termination, but don't block forever.\n            var completedTask = await Task.WhenAny(_backgroundTask, Task.Delay(5000)).ConfigureAwait(false);\n            if (completedTask != _backgroundTask)\n            {\n                // Force task to fault for diagnostic purposes\n                _metricStream.OnError(new TimeoutException(\"Aggregator failed to shut down in timely manner.\"));\n            }\n\n            _cts.Dispose();\n            _metricStream.Dispose();\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Aggregation delegate convenience builder that computes a simple\n        /// sum over NumericValue field.\n        /// </summary>\n        public static KpiAggregationDelegate SumNumeric(string metricName, Func<ProductivityEvent, bool>? predicate = null)\n            => (events, start, end) =>\n            {\n                var query = predicate is null ? events : events.Where(predicate);\n                var total = query.Sum(e => e.NumericValue.GetValueOrDefault());\n                return new[]\n                {\n                    new KpiMetric(start, end, metricName, total, DimensionKeys: null)\n                };\n            };\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Bundles aggregation related failures so they can be handled explicitly\n    /// by an upstream error boundary (e.g. Lambda DLQ).\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class AggregationException : Exception\n    {\n        public AggregationException(string message, Exception innerException)\n            : base(message, innerException)\n        {\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Example usage wiring in a Lambda context\n    //\n    // var options = new TimeWindowOptions\n    // {\n    //     WindowSize = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5),\n    //     SlideInterval = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(1)\n    // };\n    //\n    // var aggregator = new TimeSeriesWindowAggregator(\n    //         TimeSeriesWindowAggregator.SumNumeric(\"LinesOfCodeChanged\"),\n    //         options);\n    //\n    // aggregator.Subscribe(metric =>\n    // {\n    //     // e.g. Push to DynamoDB/ S3 / CloudWatch\n    //     PutMetric(metric);\n    // });\n    //\n    // inputEvents.ForEach(aggregator.OnNext);\n    // aggregator.OnCompleted();\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_75.txt",
    "numLines": 391,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 391
  },
  "type": "text"
}
53. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Search for buffer/stream race condition",
  "prompt": "Search the /app/project codebase thoroughly for any of the following patterns that could cause a race condition in a parallel processing context:\n\n1. A non-thread-safe `Queue<T>` (not ConcurrentQueue) being used across multiple threads\n2. A shared/static MemoryStream, buffer, or stream pool that could be accessed by multiple threads\n3. A class called BufferManager, StreamPool, or similar that manages reusable streams/buffers\n4. Any serialization helper that reuses a shared stream/buffer without proper synchronization\n5. Any pattern where GetStream/ReturnStream or similar acquire/release methods exist\n6. Any code where a shared object (stream, buffer, writer) is used inside a Parallel.ForEach or parallel processing context\n\nThe task description mentions:\n- A BufferManager class that pools MemoryStream objects\n- GetStream/ReturnStream methods\n- An underlying Queue<MemoryStream> used for the pool that is NOT thread-safe\n- The issue happens during Parallel.ForEach processing\n\nSearch thoroughly and report any suspicious patterns or the exact file and code that matches these descriptions.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "ab37a29",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Based on my thorough search, I need to create a comprehensive report of my findings. Let me document what I found:\n\n## RACE CONDITION ANALYSIS REPORT\n\nBased on my comprehensive search of the `/app/project` codebase, here are my findings regarding potential race conditions in parallel processing contexts:\n\n### FINDINGS SUMMARY\n\nI conducted an exhaustive search through all 90 source modules and related files looking for the specific patterns mentioned in your task description. While I did NOT find an explicit \"BufferManager\" class with non-thread-safe Queue<MemoryStream> pooling pattern, I identified several important threading-related patterns and one potential concern:\n\n---\n\n### 1. **Non-Thread-Safe List Used in Parallel Context** \n**File:** `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` (Lines 308-322)  \n**Class:** `MemoryMetricSink`  \n**Severity:** POTENTIAL RACE CONDITION\n\n```csharp\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();  // NON-THREAD-SAFE\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        _buffer.AddRange(metrics);  // UNSAFE: List<T> is not thread-safe\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n**Issue:** This class uses a non-thread-safe `List<MetricDataPoint>` field that could be accessed concurrently by multiple threads in the `EventTransformationPipeline` (module_65, lines 328-461), which uses `TransformManyBlock` with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism = 4` (or higher).\n\n---\n\n### 2. **Thread-Safe Implementations (Correctly Handled)**\n\nThe following modules properly handle parallel processing with thread-safe collections:\n\n- **Module_67.txt**: Uses `ConcurrentBag<TransformationResult>` in `Parallel.ForEachAsync` \u2705\n- **Module_72.txt**: Uses `ConcurrentQueue<Amazon.Kinesis.Model.PutRecordsRequestEntry>` \u2705\n- **Module_75.txt**: Uses `ConcurrentQueue<ProductivityEvent>` with proper ConcurrentQueue usage \u2705\n- **Module_19.txt**: Uses `lock (gate)` for synchronization on shared List in `Parallel.ForEachAsync` \u2705\n- **Module_36.txt**: Uses `lock (results)` for thread-safe access in `Parallel.ForEachAsync` \u2705\n\n---\n\n### 3. **Patterns Found But Not Race-Condition-Prone**\n\nSeveral modules use shared streams and buffers but do so safely:\n\n- **Module_46.txt**: Creates new `MemoryStream` instances per record (line 111) - no pooling\n- **Module_72.txt**: Creates new `MemoryStream` per record (line 374) - no pooling  \n- **Module_26.txt**: Uses `StreamReader` with `leaveOpen: true` but properly awaits operations\n\n---\n\n### 4. **Key Observations**\n\n1. **No \"BufferManager\" class found** - The codebase doesn't contain a class explicitly named `BufferManager` or with the GetStream/ReturnStream pooling pattern you described.\n\n2. **Most parallel processing correctly implemented** - The majority of parallel processing code (in Parallel.ForEachAsync contexts) uses either:\n   - `ConcurrentBag<T>` / `ConcurrentQueue<T>` / `ConcurrentDictionary<K,V>`\n   - Explicit `lock` statements for synchronization\n   - Proper SemaphoreSlim for throttling\n\n3. **MemoryStream usage pattern** - The code creates fresh `MemoryStream` instances rather than pooling them, avoiding the pooling race condition entirely.\n\n---\n\n### RECOMMENDATION\n\n**The `MemoryMetricSink` class in module_65.txt should be fixed** by replacing the non-thread-safe `List<T>` with a thread-safe alternative:\n\n```csharp\n// UNSAFE - Current:\nprivate readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n// SAFE - Should be:\nprivate readonly ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n```\n\nThis is the only instance I found where a non-thread-safe collection is being used in a context where it could be accessed by multiple threads.\n\n---\n\n### FILES ANALYZED\n\n- Total modules searched: 90\n- Lines of code analyzed: ~20,000+\n- Specific parallel processing patterns found: 12+\n- Thread-safe implementations: 11/12 (92% compliance)\n- Unsafe patterns identified: 1\n\nAll file paths are absolute as requested.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Search the /app/project codebase thoroughly for any of the following patterns that could cause a race condition in a parallel processing context:\n\n1. A non-thread-safe `Queue<T>` (not ConcurrentQueue) being used across multiple threads\n2. A shared/static MemoryStream, buffer, or stream pool that could be accessed by multiple threads\n3. A class called BufferManager, StreamPool, or similar that manages reusable streams/buffers\n4. Any serialization helper that reuses a shared stream/buffer without proper synchronization\n5. Any pattern where GetStream/ReturnStream or similar acquire/release methods exist\n6. Any code where a shared object (stream, buffer, writer) is used inside a Parallel.ForEach or parallel processing context\n\nThe task description mentions:\n- A BufferManager class that pools MemoryStream objects\n- GetStream/ReturnStream methods\n- An underlying Queue<MemoryStream> used for the pool that is NOT thread-safe\n- The issue happens during Parallel.ForEach processing\n\nSearch thoroughly and report any suspicious patterns or the exact file and code that matches these descriptions.",

"     1\u2192```csharp\n     2\u2192// File: src/utils.txt\n     3\u2192// NOTE: The file extension is intentionally *.txt to satisfy project-generation tooling.\n     4\u2192//       Down-stream build steps rename it to *.cs before compilation.\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192using System;\n     7\u2192using System.Buffers;\n     8\u2192using System.Collections.Generic;\n     9\u2192using System.Net;\n    10\u2192using System.Security.Cryptography;\n    11\u2192using System.Text;\n    12\u2192using System.Text.Json;\n    13\u2192using System.Text.Json.Serialization;\n    14\u2192using System.Threading;\n    15\u2192using System.Threading.Tasks;\n    16\u2192using Amazon.DynamoDBv2;\n    17\u2192using Amazon.DynamoDBv2.Model;\n    18\u2192using Amazon.Runtime;\n    19\u2192using Amazon.Runtime.Internal;\n    20\u2192using Amazon.Runtime.Internal.Transform;\n    21\u2192using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\n    22\u2192using Polly;\n    23\u2192using Polly.Contrib.WaitAndRetry;\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192namespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Common\n    26\u2192{\n    27\u2192    /// <summary>\n    28\u2192    /// A bag of high-leverage, production-ready utilities that are shared among the\n    29\u2192    /// PulseOps Warehouse Lambda micro-components.  \n    30\u2192    ///  \u2022 Structured logging helpers  \n    31\u2192    ///  \u2022 Polly-based retry policy with full-jitter exponential back-off  \n    32\u2192    ///  \u2022 System.Text.Json configuration & converters  \n    33\u2192    ///  \u2022 Idempotency primitives (hashing + DynamoDB conditional write)  \n    34\u2192    ///  \u2022 Generic batching & pagination helpers  \n    35\u2192    /// </summary>\n    36\u2192    public static class Utils\n    37\u2192    {\n    38\u2192        #region ---------- JSON ----------\n    39\u2192        private static readonly Lazy<JsonSerializerOptions> _lazyJsonOptions = new(CreateDefaultJsonOptions);\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192        /// <summary>\n    42\u2192        /// Lazily materialized, project-wide <see cref=\"JsonSerializerOptions\"/> instance.\n    43\u2192        /// Use this instance everywhere to guarantee canonical wire formats.\n    44\u2192        /// </summary>\n    45\u2192        public static JsonSerializerOptions JsonOptions => _lazyJsonOptions.Value;\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192        private static JsonSerializerOptions CreateDefaultJsonOptions()\n    48\u2192        {\n    49\u2192            var opts = new JsonSerializerOptions\n    50\u2192            {\n    51\u2192                PropertyNamingPolicy            = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,\n    52\u2192                DefaultIgnoreCondition          = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull,\n    53\u2192                WriteIndented                   = false,\n    54\u2192                AllowTrailingCommas            = true,\n    55\u2192                Converters =\n    56\u2192                {\n    57\u2192                    new JsonStringEnumConverter(JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase),\n    58\u2192                    new DateTimeUtcConverter()\n    59\u2192                }\n    60\u2192            };\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192            return opts;\n    63\u2192        }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192        /// <summary>\n    66\u2192        /// Serializes the supplied value using the shared <see cref=\"JsonOptions\"/>.\n    67\u2192        /// </summary>\n    68\u2192        public static string ToJson<T>(this T value) =>\n    69\u2192            JsonSerializer.Serialize(value, JsonOptions);\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192        /// <summary>\n    72\u2192        /// Deserializes the supplied JSON payload using the shared <see cref=\"JsonOptions\"/>.\n    73\u2192        /// </summary>\n    74\u2192        public static T? FromJson<T>(this string json) =>\n    75\u2192            JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(json, JsonOptions);\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192        private sealed class DateTimeUtcConverter : JsonConverter<DateTime>\n    78\u2192        {\n    79\u2192            public override DateTime Read(ref Utf8JsonReader reader, Type typeToConvert, JsonSerializerOptions options)\n    80\u2192            {\n    81\u2192                var dt = reader.GetDateTime();\n    82\u2192                return dt.Kind == DateTimeKind.Utc\n    83\u2192                    ? dt\n    84\u2192                    : DateTime.SpecifyKind(dt, DateTimeKind.Utc);\n    85\u2192            }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192            public override void Write(Utf8JsonWriter writer, DateTime value, JsonSerializerOptions options)\n    88\u2192            {\n    89\u2192                writer.WriteStringValue(value.ToUniversalTime());\n    90\u2192            }\n    91\u2192        }\n    92\u2192        #endregion\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192        #region ---------- LOGGING ----------\n    95\u2192        /// <summary>\n    96\u2192        /// Emits a DEBUG log with the object's JSON representation.\n    97\u2192        /// </summary>\n    98\u2192        public static void LogDebugAsJson<T>(this ILogger logger, string message, T obj)\n    99\u2192        {\n   100\u2192            if (logger.IsEnabled(LogLevel.Debug))\n   101\u2192            {\n   102\u2192                logger.LogDebug(\"{Message} | Payload={Payload}\", message, obj.ToJson());\n   103\u2192            }\n   104\u2192        }\n   105\u2192        #endregion\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192        #region ---------- RETRY POLICY ----------\n   108\u2192        /// <summary>\n   109\u2192        /// Creates a flexible Polly async retry policy with the following characteristics:\n   110\u2192        ///  \u2022 Full-jitter exponential back-off (AWS Architecture best practice)  \n   111\u2192        ///  \u2022 Retries Amazon throttling or transient network glitches (5XX, 429, RequestLimitExceeded, etc.)  \n   112\u2192        ///  \u2022 Propagates original exception once retry budget is depleted  \n   113\u2192        /// </summary>\n   114\u2192        public static IAsyncPolicy CreateAwsRetryPolicy(\n   115\u2192            ILogger logger,\n   116\u2192            int maxRetries                         = 5,\n   117\u2192            Func<int, TimeSpan>? sleepDurations    = null)\n   118\u2192        {\n   119\u2192            sleepDurations ??= retryAttempt => Backoff.FullJitterBackoff(\n   120\u2192                medianFirstRetryDelay: TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250),\n   121\u2192                retryCount:            maxRetries)[retryAttempt];\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192            bool TransientAwsError(Exception e) =>\n   124\u2192                e switch\n   125\u2192                {\n   126\u2192                    AmazonServiceException ase when\n   127\u2192                        ase.StatusCode is HttpStatusCode.TooManyRequests\n   128\u2192                                         or HttpStatusCode.InternalServerError\n   129\u2192                                         or HttpStatusCode.BadGateway\n   130\u2192                                         or HttpStatusCode.ServiceUnavailable\n   131\u2192                                         or HttpStatusCode.GatewayTimeout\n   132\u2192                        => true,\n   133\u2192                    WebException => true,\n   134\u2192                    _             => false\n   135\u2192                };\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192            return Policy\n   138\u2192                .Handle<Exception>(TransientAwsError)\n   139\u2192                .WaitAndRetryAsync(\n   140\u2192                    retryCount: maxRetries,\n   141\u2192                    sleepDurationProvider: sleepDurations,\n   142\u2192                    onRetry: (ex, ts, attempt, ctx) =>\n   143\u2192                    {\n   144\u2192                        logger.LogWarning(\n   145\u2192                            ex,\n   146\u2192                            \"Transient AWS error detected \u2014 backing off {Delay} (attempt {Attempt}/{Max})\",\n   147\u2192                            ts, attempt, maxRetries);\n   148\u2192                    });\n   149\u2192        }\n   150\u2192        #endregion\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192        #region ---------- IDEMPOTENCY ----------\n   153\u2192        private const string IdempotencyTableName = \"pulseops_idempotency\";\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192        /// <summary>\n   156\u2192        /// Generates a SHA-256 hash for <paramref name=\"payload\"/>.  \n   157\u2192        /// By default the resulting 32-byte hash is returned as unpadded Base64 to be compact yet URL-safe.\n   158\u2192        /// </summary>\n   159\u2192        public static string ComputeDeterministicHash(ReadOnlySpan<byte> payload, bool base64 = true)\n   160\u2192        {\n   161\u2192            Span<byte> hash      = stackalloc byte[32]; // SHA-256\n   162\u2192            bool rented          = false;\n   163\u2192            Span<byte> buffer    = payload;\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192            if (!payload.TryCopyTo(hash)) // Rare path \u2014 very large payload, rent array\n   166\u2192            {\n   167\u2192                rented  = true;\n   168\u2192                var arr = ArrayPool<byte>.Shared.Rent(payload.Length);\n   169\u2192                payload.CopyTo(arr);\n   170\u2192                buffer = arr.AsSpan(0, payload.Length);\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192                using var sha = SHA256.Create();\n   173\u2192                sha.TryComputeHash(buffer, hash, out _);\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192                ArrayPool<byte>.Shared.Return(arr);\n   176\u2192            }\n   177\u2192            else\n   178\u2192            {\n   179\u2192                using var sha = SHA256.Create();\n   180\u2192                sha.TryComputeHash(buffer, hash, out _);\n   181\u2192            }\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192            return base64\n   184\u2192                ? Convert.ToBase64String(hash).TrimEnd('=')\n   185\u2192                : Convert.ToHexString(hash);\n   186\u2192        }\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192        /// <summary>\n   189\u2192        /// Attempts to register an idempotent execution in DynamoDB.\n   190\u2192        /// If the <paramref name=\"hash\"/> already exists, <c>false</c> is returned and callers are\n   191\u2192        /// expected to SHORT-CIRCUIT, guaranteeing \"exactly-once\" semantics.\n   192\u2192        /// </summary>\n   193\u2192        public static async Task<bool> TryRegisterExecutionAsync(\n   194\u2192            this IAmazonDynamoDB dynamo,\n   195\u2192            string               hash,\n   196\u2192            TimeSpan             ttl,\n   197\u2192            CancellationToken    ct  = default)\n   198\u2192        {\n   199\u2192            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(hash);\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192            var nowEpochSeconds = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeSeconds();\n   202\u2192            var ttlEpoch        = nowEpochSeconds + (long)ttl.TotalSeconds;\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192            var request = new PutItemRequest\n   205\u2192            {\n   206\u2192                TableName = IdempotencyTableName,\n   207\u2192                Item = new Dictionary<string, AttributeValue>\n   208\u2192                {\n   209\u2192                    [\"pk\"]   = new() { S = hash },\n   210\u2192                    [\"ttl\"]  = new() { N = ttlEpoch.ToString() }\n   211\u2192                },\n   212\u2192                ConditionExpression = \"attribute_not_exists(pk)\"\n   213\u2192            };\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192            try\n   216\u2192            {\n   217\u2192                await dynamo.PutItemAsync(request, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   218\u2192                return true; // Successfully registered\n   219\u2192            }\n   220\u2192            catch (ConditionalCheckFailedException)\n   221\u2192            {\n   222\u2192                return false; // Duplicate invocation\n   223\u2192            }\n   224\u2192        }\n   225\u2192        #endregion\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192        #region ---------- BATCHING ----------\n   228\u2192        /// <summary>\n   229\u2192        /// Splits an <see cref=\"IEnumerable{T}\"/> into equally sized chunks.\n   230\u2192        /// </summary>\n   231\u2192        public static IEnumerable<IReadOnlyList<T>> Chunkify<T>(\n   232\u2192            this IEnumerable<T> source,\n   233\u2192            int chunkSize)\n   234\u2192        {\n   235\u2192            if (chunkSize <= 0)\n   236\u2192                throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(chunkSize), \"Chunk size must be > 0.\");\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192            using var enumerator = source.GetEnumerator();\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192            while (enumerator.MoveNext())\n   241\u2192            {\n   242\u2192                var batch = new List<T>(capacity: chunkSize) { enumerator.Current };\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192                for (int i = 1; i < chunkSize && enumerator.MoveNext(); i++)\n   245\u2192                    batch.Add(enumerator.Current);\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192                yield return batch;\n   248\u2192            }\n   249\u2192        }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192        /// <summary>\n   252\u2192        /// Consumes an async sequence (or API that returns Task<IEnumerable{T}>) page-by-page\n   253\u2192        /// and aggregates the items into <paramref name=\"buffer\"/> until the <paramref name=\"predicate\"/> returns <c>false</c>.\n   254\u2192        /// A typical use-case is DynamoDB paginate-until-consumed pattern.\n   255\u2192        /// </summary>\n   256\u2192        public static async Task<List<T>> CollectWhileAsync<T>(\n   257\u2192            Func<string?, Task<(IReadOnlyList<T> Page, string? NextToken)>> pageLoader,\n   258\u2192            Predicate<T> predicate,\n   259\u2192            List<T>? buffer = null,\n   260\u2192            CancellationToken ct = default)\n   261\u2192        {\n   262\u2192            buffer ??= new List<T>();\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192            string? token = null;\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192            do\n   267\u2192            {\n   268\u2192                ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192                var (page, next) = await pageLoader(token).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192                foreach (var item in page)\n   273\u2192                {\n   274\u2192                    if (!predicate(item))\n   275\u2192                        return buffer;\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192                    buffer.Add(item);\n   278\u2192                }\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192                token = next;\n   281\u2192            }\n   282\u2192            while (token is not null);\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192            return buffer;\n   285\u2192        }\n   286\u2192        #endregion\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192        #region ---------- TIME ----------\n   289\u2192        /// <summary>\n   290\u2192        /// Floors the given <see cref=\"DateTime\"/> down to the nearest minute boundary.\n   291\u2192        /// </summary>\n   292\u2192        public static DateTime FloorToMinute(this DateTime dt) =>\n   293\u2192            new(dt.Ticks - (dt.Ticks % TimeSpan.TicksPerMinute), DateTimeKind.Utc);\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192        /// <summary>\n   296\u2192        /// Floors the given <see cref=\"DateTime\"/> down by <paramref name=\"quantum\"/> interval.\n   297\u2192        /// Commonly used to bucket temporal metrics (e.g., 5-minute focus windows).\n   298\u2192        /// </summary>\n   299\u2192        public static DateTime Floor(this DateTime dt, TimeSpan quantum)\n   300\u2192        {\n   301\u2192            if (dt.Kind != DateTimeKind.Utc)\n   302\u2192                dt = dt.ToUniversalTime();\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192            long ticks = dt.Ticks / quantum.Ticks * quantum.Ticks;\n   305\u2192            return new DateTime(ticks, DateTimeKind.Utc);\n   306\u2192        }\n   307\u2192        #endregion\n   308\u2192    }\n   309\u2192}\n   310\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
70. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_8.txt"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```csharp\n     2\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n     3\u2192//  Project : PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n     4\u2192//  File    : src/module_8.txt         (logical source file: Module_8.cs)\n     5\u2192//  Author  : PulseOps Engineering Team\n     6\u2192//  Purpose : Implements the dynamic Transformation-Strategy runtime that powers\n     7\u2192//            pluggable KPI calculations inside the analytics fabric.\n     8\u2192//            Exposes:\n     9\u2192//              \u2022 ITransformationStrategy  \u2013 canonical strategy contract\n    10\u2192//              \u2022 TransformationStrategyAttribute \u2013 declarative registration\n    11\u2192//              \u2022 StrategyRegistry         \u2013 reflection-based discovery/DI glue\n    12\u2192//              \u2022 TransformationExecutor   \u2013 concurrent orchestration & telemetry\n    13\u2192//              \u2022 Example concrete strategy (FocusTimeRatioStrategy)\n    14\u2192//\n    15\u2192//  Note    : Although this file has a .txt path suffix in the repository, the\n    16\u2192//            build pipeline aliases it to a .cs compilation unit. This trick\n    17\u2192//            enables litterate-spec documentation tooling downstream.\n    18\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192using System;\n    21\u2192using System.Collections.Concurrent;\n    22\u2192using System.Collections.Generic;\n    23\u2192using System.Diagnostics;\n    24\u2192using System.Linq;\n    25\u2192using System.Reflection;\n    26\u2192using System.Text.Json;\n    27\u2192using System.Threading;\n    28\u2192using System.Threading.Tasks;\n    29\u2192using Amazon.CloudWatch;\n    30\u2192using Amazon.CloudWatch.Model;\n    31\u2192using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\n    32\u2192using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions;\n    33\u2192using PulseOps.Warehouse.Shared;\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192// ReSharper disable MemberCanBePrivate.Global\n    36\u2192// ReSharper disable UnusedType.Global\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192namespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Transformations\n    39\u2192{\n    40\u2192    #region \u2014\u2014\u2014 Contracts \u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\u2014\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    /// <summary>\n    43\u2192    /// Canonical contract that every KPI transformation strategy must implement.\n    44\u2192    /// </summary>\n    45\u2192    /// <typeparam name=\"TIn\">Normalized input record.</typeparam>\n    46\u2192    /// <typeparam name=\"TOut\">Enriched / calculated record.</typeparam>\n    47\u2192    public interface ITransformationStrategy<in TIn, TOut>\n    48\u2192    {\n    49\u2192        /// <summary>\n    50\u2192        /// Executes the strategy asynchronously.\n    51\u2192        /// </summary>\n    52\u2192        /// <param name=\"input\">Stream (enumerable) of incoming records.</param>\n    53\u2192        /// <param name=\"context\">Request-scoped metadata, DI container, etc.</param>\n    54\u2192        /// <param name=\"cancellationToken\">Co-operative cancellation token.</param>\n    55\u2192        /// <returns>Transformed records.</returns>\n    56\u2192        IAsyncEnumerable<TOut> TransformAsync(\n    57\u2192            IAsyncEnumerable<TIn> input,\n    58\u2192            TransformationContext context,\n    59\u2192            CancellationToken cancellationToken = default);\n    60\u2192    }\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    /// <summary>\n    63\u2192    /// Attribute used to declaratively register a strategy implementation.\n    64\u2192    /// </summary>\n    65\u2192    [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class, AllowMultiple = false)]\n    66\u2192    public sealed class TransformationStrategyAttribute : Attribute\n    67\u2192    {\n    68\u2192        public TransformationStrategyAttribute(string name, string version = \"1.0\")\n    69\u2192        {\n    70\u2192            Name    = name ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(name));\n    71\u2192            Version = version;\n    72\u2192        }\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192        public string Name    { get; }\n    75\u2192        public
