"     1\u2192```csharp\n     2\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n     3\u2192//  File:        module_65.txt  (logical C# source file)\n     4\u2192//  Project:     PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n     5\u2192//  Description: Shared transformation engine & strategies for converting raw\n     6\u2192//               WorkActivityEvent records into normalized MetricDataPoint(s).\n     7\u2192//               Demonstrates Strategy-, Pipeline- and Observer-Patterns,\n     8\u2192//               asynchronous data-flow, structured logging, and resilience.\n     9\u2192//  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    10\u2192//  NOTE: This is a single-file delivery for brevity.  In the real repository\n    11\u2192//        the types would be split into discrete files/namespaces.\n    12\u2192//\n    13\u2192//  External NuGet dependencies assumed:\n    14\u2192//\n    15\u2192//      <PackageReference Include=\"Serilog\" Version=\"2.*\" />\n    16\u2192//      <PackageReference Include=\"Serilog.Sinks.Console\" Version=\"4.*\" />\n    17\u2192//      <PackageReference Include=\"System.Threading.Tasks.Dataflow\" Version=\"6.*\" />\n    18\u2192//      <PackageReference Include=\"System.Text.Json\" Version=\"7.*\" />\n    19\u2192//\n    20\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192#nullable enable\n    23\u2192using System;\n    24\u2192using System.Collections.Generic;\n    25\u2192using System.Collections.Immutable;\n    26\u2192using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;\n    27\u2192using System.Globalization;\n    28\u2192using System.Text.Json;\n    29\u2192using System.Text.Json.Serialization;\n    30\u2192using System.Threading;\n    31\u2192using System.Threading.Tasks;\n    32\u2192using System.Threading.Tasks.Dataflow;\n    33\u2192using Serilog;\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192namespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Transformations\n    36\u2192{\n    37\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    38\u2192    //  Domain primitives\n    39\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    /// <summary>\n    42\u2192    /// Logical activity categories arriving from the ingestion layer.\n    43\u2192    /// </summary>\n    44\u2192    public enum ActivityType\n    45\u2192    {\n    46\u2192        Unknown  = 0,\n    47\u2192        Meeting  = 1,\n    48\u2192        Focus    = 2,\n    49\u2192        Break    = 3,\n    50\u2192        Coding   = 4,\n    51\u2192        Review   = 5\n    52\u2192    }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    /// <summary>\n    55\u2192    /// Canonical event record produced by the ingestion Lambda(s).\n    56\u2192    /// Represents a single contiguous block of work a user performed.\n    57\u2192    /// </summary>\n    58\u2192    /// <param name=\"UserId\">Opaque identifier for the user (hashed e-mail).</param>\n    59\u2192    /// <param name=\"StartUtc\">Inclusive start timestamp.</param>\n    60\u2192    /// <param name=\"EndUtc\">Exclusive end timestamp.</param>\n    61\u2192    /// <param name=\"Activity\">High-level category of activity.</param>\n    62\u2192    /// <param name=\"Metadata\">\n    63\u2192    /// Additional JSON metadata produced by the source system\n    64\u2192    /// (e.g. Zoom/Teams meeting payload, GitHub commit info, etc.).\n    65\u2192    /// </param>\n    66\u2192    public sealed record WorkActivityEvent(\n    67\u2192        string                  UserId,\n    68\u2192        DateTimeOffset          StartUtc,\n    69\u2192        DateTimeOffset          EndUtc,\n    70\u2192        ActivityType            Activity,\n    71\u2192        string?                 Metadata = null)\n    72\u2192    {\n    73\u2192        public TimeSpan Duration => EndUtc - StartUtc;\n    74\u2192    }\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    /// <summary>\n    77\u2192    /// Normalized metric datapoint emitted by transformation strategies.\n    78\u2192    /// </summary>\n    79\u2192    public sealed record MetricDataPoint(\n    80\u2192        string                      UserId,\n    81\u2192        string                      MetricName,\n    82\u2192        double                      Value,\n    83\u2192        DateTimeOffset              Timestamp,\n    84\u2192        IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> Dimensions);\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    87\u2192    //  Contracts for transformation pipeline\n    88\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    /// <summary>\n    91\u2192    /// Computes metrics from a WorkActivityEvent.\n    92\u2192    /// Adheres to the Strategy pattern for plug-n-play business logic.\n    93\u2192    /// </summary>\n    94\u2192    public interface IMetricComputationStrategy\n    95\u2192    {\n    96\u2192        /// <summary>\n    97\u2192        /// Returns <c>true</c> if this strategy can handle the given event.\n    98\u2192        /// </summary>\n    99\u2192        bool CanHandle(in WorkActivityEvent workEvent);\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192        /// <summary>\n   102\u2192        /// Performs the metric computation.\n   103\u2192        /// May emit zero or more MetricDataPoint records.\n   104\u2192        /// </summary>\n   105\u2192        ValueTask<IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint>> ComputeAsync(\n   106\u2192            WorkActivityEvent workEvent,\n   107\u2192            CancellationToken cancellationToken = default);\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192    /// <summary>\n   111\u2192    /// Consumer of computed metrics \u2014 e.g. Kinesis Firehose, CloudWatch, Prometheus.\n   112\u2192    /// </summary>\n   113\u2192    public interface IMetricSink\n   114\u2192    {\n   115\u2192        Task WriteAsync(\n   116\u2192            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n   117\u2192            CancellationToken                    cancellationToken = default);\n   118\u2192    }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   121\u2192    //  Concrete strategies\n   122\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    /// <summary>\n   125\u2192    /// Strategy that emits raw focus-time minutes for Focus activities.\n   126\u2192    /// Additional ratio aggregation is done downstream in Athena/QuickSight.\n   127\u2192    /// </summary>\n   128\u2192    public sealed class FocusTimeStrategy : IMetricComputationStrategy\n   129\u2192    {\n   130\u2192        private static readonly MetricDataPoint[] Empty = Array.Empty<MetricDataPoint>();\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192        public bool CanHandle(in WorkActivityEvent workEvent)\n   133\u2192            => workEvent.Activity == ActivityType.Focus;\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192        public ValueTask<IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint>> ComputeAsync(\n   136\u2192            WorkActivityEvent workEvent,\n   137\u2192            CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n   138\u2192        {\n   139\u2192            double minutes = workEvent.Duration.TotalMinutes;\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192            if (minutes <= 0)\n   142\u2192            {\n   143\u2192                return ValueTask.FromResult<IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint>>(Empty);\n   144\u2192            }\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192            var dp = new MetricDataPoint(\n   147\u2192                workEvent.UserId,\n   148\u2192                MetricName: \"focus_time_minutes\",\n   149\u2192                Value: Math.Round(minutes, 2, MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero),\n   150\u2192                Timestamp: workEvent.EndUtc,\n   151\u2192                Dimensions: new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.Ordinal)\n   152\u2192                {\n   153\u2192                    [\"activity_type\"] = workEvent.Activity.ToString()\n   154\u2192                });\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192            return ValueTask.FromResult<IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint>>(\n   157\u2192                new[] { dp });\n   158\u2192        }\n   159\u2192    }\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192    /// <summary>\n   162\u2192    /// Strategy that calculates the total cost of a meeting based on its\n   163\u2192    /// duration and participants\u2019 assumed hourly rate injected through metadata.\n   164\u2192    /// </summary>\n   165\u2192    public sealed class MeetingCostIndexStrategy : IMetricComputationStrategy\n   166\u2192    {\n   167\u2192        private static readonly MetricDataPoint[] Empty = Array.Empty<MetricDataPoint>();\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192        private readonly double _defaultHourlyRateUsd;\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192        public MeetingCostIndexStrategy(double defaultHourlyRateUsd = 75)\n   172\u2192        {\n   173\u2192            if (defaultHourlyRateUsd <= 0)\n   174\u2192                throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(\n   175\u2192                    nameof(defaultHourlyRateUsd),\n   176\u2192                    \"Hourly rate must be positive.\");\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192            _defaultHourlyRateUsd = defaultHourlyRateUsd;\n   179\u2192        }\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192        public bool CanHandle(in WorkActivityEvent workEvent)\n   182\u2192            => workEvent.Activity == ActivityType.Meeting;\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192        public async ValueTask<IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint>> ComputeAsync(\n   185\u2192            WorkActivityEvent workEvent,\n   186\u2192            CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n   187\u2192        {\n   188\u2192            // Guard against malformed events early; no metrics emitted.\n   189\u2192            if (workEvent.Duration <= TimeSpan.Zero)\n   190\u2192                return Empty;\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192            // Extract meeting metadata (participants, rate override, etc.)\n   193\u2192            int participantCount = 0;\n   194\u2192            double? hourlyRateOverride = null;\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(workEvent.Metadata))\n   197\u2192            {\n   198\u2192                try\n   199\u2192                {\n   200\u2192                    var meetingMeta = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<MeetingMetadata>(\n   201\u2192                        workEvent.Metadata,\n   202\u2192                        SerializerOptions.Default);\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192                    if (meetingMeta is not null)\n   205\u2192                    {\n   206\u2192                        participantCount   = meetingMeta.ParticipantCount ?? participantCount;\n   207\u2192                        hourlyRateOverride = meetingMeta.HourlyRateUsd;\n   208\u2192                    }\n   209\u2192                }\n   210\u2192                catch (JsonException ex)\n   211\u2192                {\n   212\u2192                    Log.Warning(\n   213\u2192                        ex,\n   214\u2192                        \"Failed to deserialize meeting metadata for event {EventId}. Raw: {Raw}\",\n   215\u2192                        workEvent.GetHashCode(),\n   216\u2192                        workEvent.Metadata);\n   217\u2192                }\n   218\u2192            }\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192            participantCount = Math.Max(participantCount, 1); // At least one person.\n   221\u2192            double hourlyRate = hourlyRateOverride ?? _defaultHourlyRateUsd;\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192            double costUsd = participantCount *\n   224\u2192                             hourlyRate *\n   225\u2192                             workEvent.Duration.TotalHours;\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192            var dp = new MetricDataPoint(\n   228\u2192                workEvent.UserId,\n   229\u2192                MetricName: \"meeting_cost_usd\",\n   230\u2192                Value: Math.Round(costUsd, 2, MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero),\n   231\u2192                Timestamp: workEvent.EndUtc,\n   232\u2192                Dimensions: new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.Ordinal)\n   233\u2192                {\n   234\u2192                    [\"participant_count\"] = participantCount.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture),\n   235\u2192                    [\"hourly_rate\"]       = hourlyRate.ToString(\"F2\", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)\n   236\u2192                });\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192            return new[] { dp };\n   239\u2192        }\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192        // Internal DTO to map metadata JSON.\n   242\u2192        private sealed record MeetingMetadata\n   243\u2192        {\n   244\u2192            [JsonPropertyName(\"participantCount\")]\n   245\u2192            public int?   ParticipantCount { get; init; }\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192            [JsonPropertyName(\"hourlyRateUsd\")]\n   248\u2192            public double? HourlyRateUsd   { get; init; }\n   249\u2192        }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192        // Re-usable serializer options.\n   252\u2192        private static class SerializerOptions\n   253\u2192        {\n   254\u2192            public static readonly JsonSerializerOptions Default = new()\n   255\u2192            {\n   256\u2192                PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,\n   257\u2192                AllowTrailingCommas         = true,\n   258\u2192                ReadCommentHandling         = JsonCommentHandling.Skip\n   259\u2192            };\n   260\u2192        }\n   261\u2192    }\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   264\u2192    //  Utilities\n   265\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192    /// <summary>\n   268\u2192    /// Registry for fast lookup of the first matching strategy.\n   269\u2192    /// </summary>\n   270\u2192    public sealed class StrategyRegistry\n   271\u2192    {\n   272\u2192        private readonly ImmutableArray<IMetricComputationStrategy> _strategies;\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192        public StrategyRegistry(IEnumerable<IMetricComputationStrategy> strategies)\n   275\u2192        {\n   276\u2192            _strategies = strategies is ImmutableArray<IMetricComputationStrategy> arr\n   277\u2192                        ? arr\n   278\u2192                        : strategies.ToImmutableArray();\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192            if (_strategies.IsDefaultOrEmpty)\n   281\u2192                throw new ArgumentException(\n   282\u2192                    \"Strategy collection cannot be empty.\",\n   283\u2192                    nameof(strategies));\n   284\u2192        }\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192        public bool TryResolve(\n   287\u2192            WorkActivityEvent            workEvent,\n   288\u2192            [NotNullWhen(true)]\n   289\u2192            out IMetricComputationStrategy? strategy)\n   290\u2192        {\n   291\u2192            foreach (var strat in _strategies)\n   292\u2192            {\n   293\u2192                if (strat.CanHandle(workEvent))\n   294\u2192                {\n   295\u2192                    strategy = strat;\n   296\u2192                    return true;\n   297\u2192                }\n   298\u2192            }\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192            strategy = null;\n   301\u2192            return false;\n   302\u2192        }\n   303\u2192    }\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192    /// <summary>\n   306\u2192    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n   307\u2192    /// </summary>\n   308\u2192    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n   309\u2192    {\n   310\u2192        private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192        public Task WriteAsync(\n   313\u2192            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n   314\u2192            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n   315\u2192        {\n   316\u2192            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n   317\u2192            return Task.CompletedTask;\n   318\u2192        }\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n   321\u2192            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n   322\u2192    }\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   325\u2192    //  Transformation pipeline (Dataflow) \u2014 high throughput, back-pressure aware\n   326\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192    public sealed class EventTransformationPipeline : IAsyncDisposable\n   329\u2192    {\n   330\u2192        private readonly BufferBlock<WorkActivityEvent>                _input;\n   331\u2192        private readonly TransformManyBlock<WorkActivityEvent, MetricDataPoint> _compute;\n   332\u2192        private readonly ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>                  _sink;\n   333\u2192        private readonly ILogger                                       _log = Log.ForContext<EventTransformationPipeline>();\n   334\u2192        private readonly CancellationTokenSource                        _cts = new();\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192        public EventTransformationPipeline(\n   337\u2192            StrategyRegistry strategyRegistry,\n   338\u2192            IMetricSink      metricSink,\n   339\u2192            int              maxDegreeOfParallelism = 4,\n   340\u2192            int              boundedCapacity        = 1_024)\n   341\u2192        {\n   342\u2192            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(strategyRegistry);\n   343\u2192            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(metricSink);\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192            // Configure blocks\n   346\u2192            _input = new BufferBlock<WorkActivityEvent>(\n   347\u2192                new DataflowBlockOptions\n   348\u2192                {\n   349\u2192                    BoundedCapacity = boundedCapacity\n   350\u2192                });\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192            _compute = new TransformManyBlock<WorkActivityEvent, MetricDataPoint>(\n   353\u2192                async (workEvent) =>\n   354\u2192                {\n   355\u2192                    try\n   356\u2192                    {\n   357\u2192                        if (strategyRegistry.TryResolve(workEvent, out var strategy))\n   358\u2192                        {\n   359\u2192                            return await strategy.ComputeAsync(\n   360\u2192                                workEvent,\n   361\u2192                                _cts.Token);\n   362\u2192                        }\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192                        _log.Debug(\"No strategy matched event {@Event}\", workEvent);\n   365\u2192                        return Array.Empty<MetricDataPoint>();\n   366\u2192                    }\n   367\u2192                    catch (Exception ex)\n   368\u2192                    {\n   369\u2192                        _log.Error(\n   370\u2192                            ex,\n   371\u2192                            \"Metric computation failed for event {@Event}\",\n   372\u2192                            workEvent);\n   373\u2192                        return Array.Empty<MetricDataPoint>();\n   374\u2192                    }\n   375\u2192                },\n   376\u2192                new ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions\n   377\u2192                {\n   378\u2192                    MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism,\n   379\u2192                    BoundedCapacity        = boundedCapacity,\n   380\u2192                    CancellationToken      = _cts.Token,\n   381\u2192                    EnsureOrdered          = false\n   382\u2192                });\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192            _sink = new ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>(\n   385\u2192                async (metric) =>\n   386\u2192                {\n   387\u2192                    try\n   388\u2192                    {\n   389\u2192                        await metricSink.WriteAsync(\n   390\u2192                            new[] { metric },\n   391\u2192                            _cts.Token);\n   392\u2192                    }\n   393\u2192                    catch (Exception ex)\n   394\u2192                    {\n   395\u2192                        _log.Error(\n   396\u2192                            ex,\n   397\u2192                            \"Failed to write metric {@Metric}\",\n   398\u2192                            metric);\n   399\u2192                        // In production, failed metrics would be forwarded to a DLQ.\n   400\u2192                    }\n   401\u2192                },\n   402\u2192                new ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions\n   403\u2192                {\n   404\u2192                    MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism,\n   405\u2192                    BoundedCapacity        = boundedCapacity,\n   406\u2192                    CancellationToken      = _cts.Token,\n   407\u2192                    EnsureOrdered          = false\n   408\u2192                });\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192            // Wire-up blocks\n   411\u2192            _input.LinkTo(_compute,                    new DataflowLinkOptions { PropagateCompletion = true });\n   412\u2192            _compute.LinkTo(_sink,                     new DataflowLinkOptions { PropagateCompletion = true });\n   413\u2192        }\n   414\u2192\n   415\u2192        /// <summary>\n   416\u2192        /// Enqueues a new event for processing.  Respects back-pressure.\n   417\u2192        /// </summary>\n   418\u2192        public async Task SubmitAsync(\n   419\u2192            WorkActivityEvent  workEvent,\n   420\u2192            CancellationToken  cancellationToken = default)\n   421\u2192        {\n   422\u2192            if (!_input.Post(workEvent))\n   423\u2192            {\n   424\u2192                await _input.SendAsync(workEvent, cancellationToken);\n   425\u2192            }\n   426\u2192        }\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192        /// <summary>\n   429\u2192        /// Signals graceful shutdown and waits until all buffered work completes.\n   430\u2192        /// </summary>\n   431\u2192        public async Task CompleteAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n   432\u2192        {\n   433\u2192            _input.Complete();\n   434\u2192            try\n   435\u2192            {\n   436\u2192                await _sink.Completion.WaitAsync(cancellationToken);\n   437\u2192            }\n   438\u2192            catch (OperationCanceledException) when (cancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested)\n   439\u2192            {\n   440\u2192                _cts.Cancel();\n   441\u2192                throw;\n   442\u2192            }\n   443\u2192        }\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192        public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()\n   446\u2192        {\n   447\u2192            _cts.Cancel();\n   448\u2192            _input.Complete();\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192            try\n   451\u2192            {\n   452\u2192                await _sink.Completion;\n   453\u2192            }\n   454\u2192            catch\n   455\u2192            {\n   456\u2192                // Swallow any in-flight exceptions on dispose.\n   457\u2192            }\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192            _cts.Dispose();\n   460\u2192        }\n   461\u2192    }\n   462\u2192\n   463\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   464\u2192    //  Example bootstrapping (would live in Lambda entrypoint or ASP.NET Startup)\n   465\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192    public static class TransformationBootstrap\n   468\u2192    {\n   469\u2192        /// <summary>\n   470\u2192        /// Configures Serilog and returns a ready-to-use pipeline for ad-hoc demos.\n   471\u2192        /// </summary>\n   472\u2192        public static EventTransformationPipeline CreateDemoPipeline()\n   473\u2192        {\n   474\u2192            Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()\n   475\u2192                .Enrich.FromLogContext()\n   476\u2192                .MinimumLevel.Debug()\n   477\u2192                .WriteTo.Console()\n   478\u2192                .CreateLogger();\n   479\u2192\n   480\u2192            var strategies = new IMetricComputationStrategy[]\n   481\u2192            {\n   482\u2192                new FocusTimeStrategy(),\n   483\u2192                new MeetingCostIndexStrategy(defaultHourlyRateUsd: 90)\n   484\u2192            };\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192            var registry   = new StrategyRegistry(strategies);\n   487\u2192            var sink       = new MemoryMetricSink();\n   488\u2192            var pipeline   = new EventTransformationPipeline(registry, sink);\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192            return pipeline;\n   491\u2192        }\n   492\u2192    }\n   493\u2192}\n   494\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

98. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_80.txt"

Output
"     1\u2192```csharp\n     2\u2192// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n     3\u2192// File:    module_80.cs                                      (original path: src/module_80.txt)\n     4\u2192// Project: PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n     5\u2192//\n     6\u2192// Purpose: High-throughput Kinesis stream processor that dynamically routes raw telemetry\n     7\u2192//          events to the correct KPI transformation strategy.  Implements a reflection-\n     8\u2192//          driven Strategy pattern, offers bounded parallelism, publishes faults to a DLQ,\n     9\u2192//          and is designed to run as an AWS Lambda function.\n    10\u2192//\n    11\u2192// NOTE:    This single file is self-contained for illustration purposes.  In the real code-\n    12\u2192//          base, each interface/class would live in its own file and be covered by unit tests.\n    13\u2192// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192#pragma warning disable CA1031 // Do not catch general exception types \u2014 deliberate for top-level fault barrier.\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192using System;\n    18\u2192using System.Collections.Concurrent;\n    19\u2192using System.Collections.Generic;\n    20\u2192using System.Linq;\n    21\u2192using System.Reflection;\n    22\u2192using System.Text;\n    23\u2192using System.Text.Json;\n    24\u2192using System.Threading;\n    25\u2192using System.Threading.Tasks;\n    26\u2192using Amazon.Lambda.Core;\n    27\u2192using Amazon.Lambda.KinesisEvents;\n    28\u2192using Amazon.SimpleNotificationService;\n    29\u2192using Amazon.SimpleNotificationService.Model;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192// ReSharper disable ClassNeverInstantiated.Global \u2014 instantiated by AWS Lambda runtime.\n    32\u2192namespace PulseOps.Warehouse.StreamProcessing\n    33\u2192{\n    34\u2192    #region === Public Lambda Entrypoint ==============================================================================\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    /// <summary>\n    37\u2192    /// Lambda entry-point for the \u201ctransform\u201d stage of the warehouse ingestion pipeline.\n    38\u2192    /// The function is subscribed to a Kinesis Data Stream fan-out of raw events.\n    39\u2192    /// Each event is dynamically dispatched to a concrete <see cref=\"ITransformationStrategy\"/>\n    40\u2192    /// based on its content-type.  KPI records are then written to an intermediary store\n    41\u2192    /// (omitted here \u2014 would typically be a Firehose stream or S3), while transformation\n    42\u2192    /// failures are routed to a DLQ via SNS.\n    43\u2192    /// </summary>\n    44\u2192    public sealed class StreamTransformationCoordinator\n    45\u2192    {\n    46\u2192        private const int DefaultParallelism = 8;            // Tune according to Lambda memory size / vCPU.\n    47\u2192        private readonly IStrategyResolver _resolver;\n    48\u2192        private readonly IDlqPublisher _dlqPublisher;\n    49\u2192        private readonly int _maxParallelism;\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192        /// <summary>\n    52\u2192        /// Parameterless constructor required by AWS Lambda runtime.  Uses DI-less\n    53\u2192        /// service locator helpers (<see cref=\"StrategyResolver\"/> and <see cref=\"SnsDlqPublisher\"/>).\n    54\u2192        /// </summary>\n    55\u2192        public StreamTransformationCoordinator()\n    56\u2192            : this(new StrategyResolver(), new SnsDlqPublisher(), DefaultParallelism)\n    57\u2192        { }\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192        /// <summary>For unit-testing / composition-root usage.</summary>\n    60\u2192        public StreamTransformationCoordinator(\n    61\u2192            IStrategyResolver resolver,\n    62\u2192            IDlqPublisher dlqPublisher,\n    63\u2192            int maxParallelism)\n    64\u2192        {\n    65\u2192            _resolver = resolver ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(resolver));\n    66\u2192            _dlqPublisher = dlqPublisher ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(dlqPublisher));\n    67\u2192            _maxParallelism = maxParallelism < 1 ? DefaultParallelism : maxParallelism;\n    68\u2192        }\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192        // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    71\u2192        // Lambda handler\n    72\u2192        // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192        /// <summary>\n    75\u2192        /// Primary Lambda handler invoked by the AWS runtime.\n    76\u2192        /// </summary>\n    77\u2192        /// <remarks>\n    78\u2192        /// Signature must remain asynchronous for the runtime to honour returned task completion.\n    79\u2192        /// </remarks>\n    80\u2192        public async Task HandleAsync(KinesisEvent kinesisEvent, ILambdaContext context)\n    81\u2192        {\n    82\u2192            var logger = context.Logger;\n    83\u2192            logger.LogDebug($\"Received batch with {kinesisEvent.Records.Count} record(s).\");\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192            using var semaphore = new SemaphoreSlim(_maxParallelism);\n    86\u2192            var tasks = kinesisEvent.Records.Select(async record =>\n    87\u2192            {\n    88\u2192                await semaphore.WaitAsync(context.CancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);\n    89\u2192                try\n    90\u2192                {\n    91\u2192                    await ProcessRecordAsync(record, context).ConfigureAwait(false);\n    92\u2192                }\n    93\u2192                finally\n    94\u2192                {\n    95\u2192                    semaphore.Release();\n    96\u2192                }\n    97\u2192            }).ToList();\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192            await Task.WhenAll(tasks).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192            logger.LogInformation(\"Batch processed successfully.\");\n   102\u2192        }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192        #endregion\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192        #region === Record Processing ===================================================================================\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192        private async Task ProcessRecordAsync(KinesisEvent.KinesisEventRecord record, ILambdaContext ctx)\n   109\u2192        {\n   110\u2192            var logger = ctx.Logger;\n   111\u2192            RawEvent rawEvent;\n   112\u2192            try\n   113\u2192            {\n   114\u2192                rawEvent = DeserializeRecord(record);\n   115\u2192            }\n   116\u2192            catch (Exception ex)\n   117\u2192            {\n   118\u2192                logger.LogError($\"[DeserializationFail] PartitionKey={record.Kinesis.PartitionKey} \u2014 {ex}\");\n   119\u2192                await _dlqPublisher.PublishAsync(DlqMessage.FromDeadLetter(record, ex), ctx.CancellationToken)\n   120\u2192                                   .ConfigureAwait(false);\n   121\u2192                return;\n   122\u2192            }\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192            ITransformationStrategy? strategy = null;\n   125\u2192            try\n   126\u2192            {\n   127\u2192                strategy = _resolver.Resolve(rawEvent.ContentType);\n   128\u2192            }\n   129\u2192            catch (UnknownContentTypeException uctEx)\n   130\u2192            {\n   131\u2192                logger.LogWarning(uctEx.Message);\n   132\u2192                await _dlqPublisher.PublishAsync(DlqMessage.FromDeadLetter(record, uctEx), ctx.CancellationToken)\n   133\u2192                                   .ConfigureAwait(false);\n   134\u2192                return;\n   135\u2192            }\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192            try\n   138\u2192            {\n   139\u2192                var kpiRecord = await strategy.TransformAsync(rawEvent, ctx.CancellationToken).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192                // Persist KPI record (omitted \u2014 could be S3, Redshift, Firehose, etc.).\n   142\u2192                logger.LogDebug($\"Successfully transformed event \u2192 KPI '{kpiRecord.KpiName}'.\");\n   143\u2192            }\n   144\u2192            catch (Exception ex)\n   145\u2192            {\n   146\u2192                logger.LogError($\"[StrategyFail] Strategy={strategy.GetType().Name} \u2014 {ex}\");\n   147\u2192                await _dlqPublisher.PublishAsync(DlqMessage.FromDeadLetter(record, ex), ctx.CancellationToken)\n   148\u2192                                   .ConfigureAwait(false);\n   149\u2192            }\n   150\u2192        }\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192        private static RawEvent DeserializeRecord(KinesisEvent.KinesisEventRecord record)\n   153\u2192        {\n   154\u2192            var json = Encoding.UTF8.GetString(record.Kinesis.Data.Span);\n   155\u2192            var document = JsonDocument.Parse(json);\n   156\u2192            var root = document.RootElement;\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192            return new RawEvent(\n   159\u2192                ContentType: root.GetProperty(\"contentType\").GetString() ?? \"application/unknown\",\n   160\u2192                Payload: root.GetProperty(\"payload\"),\n   161\u2192                Timestamp: root.GetProperty(\"timestamp\").GetDateTime());\n   162\u2192        }\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192        #endregion\n   165\u2192    }\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    #region === Strategy Pattern Infrastructure =======================================================================\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192    /// <summary>\n   170\u2192    /// Lightweight service-locator for <see cref=\"ITransformationStrategy\"/> implementations.\n   171\u2192    /// Scans the current assembly only once and caches results in a thread-safe dictionary.\n   172\u2192    /// </summary>\n   173\u2192    internal sealed class StrategyResolver : IStrategyResolver\n   174\u2192    {\n   175\u2192        private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, ITransformationStrategy> _cache = new();\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192        public StrategyResolver()\n   178\u2192        {\n   179\u2192            // Pre-warm the cache so that we fail fast on startup if multiple\n   180\u2192            // strategies claim to handle the same content type.\n   181\u2192            var implementations = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()\n   182\u2192                                          .GetTypes()\n   183\u2192                                          .Where(t => !t.IsAbstract && typeof(ITransformationStrategy).IsAssignableFrom(t));\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192            foreach (var type in implementations)\n   186\u2192            {\n   187\u2192                var instance = (ITransformationStrategy)Activator.CreateInstance(type)!;\n   188\u2192                foreach (var contentType in instance.SupportedContentTypes)\n   189\u2192                {\n   190\u2192                    if (!_cache.TryAdd(contentType, instance))\n   191\u2192                    {\n   192\u2192                        throw new InvalidOperationException(\n   193\u2192                            $\"Multiple transformation strategies registered for content type '{contentType}'.\");\n   194\u2192                    }\n   195\u2192                }\n   196\u2192            }\n   197\u2192        }\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192        public ITransformationStrategy Resolve(string contentType)\n   200\u2192        {\n   201\u2192            if (!_cache.TryGetValue(contentType, out var strategy))\n   202\u2192            {\n   203\u2192                throw new UnknownContentTypeException(contentType);\n   204\u2192            }\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192            return strategy;\n   207\u2192        }\n   208\u2192    }\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192    /// <summary>\n   211\u2192    /// Thrown when no strategy is found for the specified content type.\n   212\u2192    /// </summary>\n   213\u2192    public sealed class UnknownContentTypeException : Exception\n   214\u2192    {\n   215\u2192        public UnknownContentTypeException(string contentType)\n   216\u2192            : base($\"No transformation strategy registered for content type '{contentType}'.\")\n   217\u2192        { }\n   218\u2192    }\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192    /// <summary>\n   221\u2192    /// Abstraction for locating the correct transformation strategy.\n   222\u2192    /// </summary>\n   223\u2192    public interface IStrategyResolver\n   224\u2192    {\n   225\u2192        ITransformationStrategy Resolve(string contentType);\n   226\u2192    }\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192    /// <summary>\n   229\u2192    /// Common interface for KPI transformation strategies.  Each implementation\n   230\u2192    /// must declare which content types it can consume.\n   231\u2192    /// </summary>\n   232\u2192    public interface ITransformationStrategy\n   233\u2192    {\n   234\u2192        IReadOnlyCollection<string> SupportedContentTypes { get; }\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192        /// <summary>\n   237\u2192        /// Transforms a raw telemetry event into a normalized KPI record.\n   238\u2192        /// </summary>\n   239\u2192        Task<KpiRecord> TransformAsync(RawEvent rawEvent, CancellationToken token);\n   240\u2192    }\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192    #endregion\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192    #region === Concrete Strategy Examples ============================================================================\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192    /// <summary>\n   247\u2192    /// Example strategy that derives \u201cFocus Time\u201d KPI from calendar metadata.\n   248\u2192    /// </summary>\n   249\u2192    internal sealed class CalendarFocusTimeStrategy : ITransformationStrategy\n   250\u2192    {\n   251\u2192        public IReadOnlyCollection<string> SupportedContentTypes { get; } =\n   252\u2192            new[] { \"application/vnd.pulseops.calendar+json\" };\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192        public Task<KpiRecord> TransformAsync(RawEvent rawEvent, CancellationToken token)\n   255\u2192        {\n   256\u2192            // Domain-specific parsing stripped down for brevity.\n   257\u2192            var meetingDurationMinutes = rawEvent.Payload.GetProperty(\"meetingDurationMinutes\").GetInt32();\n   258\u2192            var userId = rawEvent.Payload.GetProperty(\"userId\").GetString()!;\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192            var focusTime = 480 - meetingDurationMinutes; // 8h work day.\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192            var kpiPayload = JsonSerializer.SerializeToElement(new\n   263\u2192            {\n   264\u2192                userId,\n   265\u2192                focusTimeMinutes = focusTime,\n   266\u2192                computationDate = rawEvent.Timestamp.Date\n   267\u2192            });\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192            return Task.FromResult(new KpiRecord(\"focus_time\", kpiPayload));\n   270\u2192        }\n   271\u2192    }\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192    /// <summary>\n   274\u2192    /// Example strategy computing \u201cDeployment Lead Time\u201d KPI from CI/CD events.\n   275\u2192    /// </summary>\n   276\u2192    internal sealed class DeploymentLeadTimeStrategy : ITransformationStrategy\n   277\u2192    {\n   278\u2192        public IReadOnlyCollection<string> SupportedContentTypes { get; } =\n   279\u2192            new[] { \"application/vnd.pulseops.cicd+json\" };\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192        public Task<KpiRecord> TransformAsync(RawEvent rawEvent, CancellationToken token)\n   282\u2192        {\n   283\u2192            var queuedAt = rawEvent.Payload.GetProperty(\"queuedAt\").GetDateTime();\n   284\u2192            var deployedAt = rawEvent.Payload.GetProperty(\"deployedAt\").GetDateTime();\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192            var leadTimeMinutes = (deployedAt - queuedAt).TotalMinutes;\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192            var pipelineId = rawEvent.Payload.GetProperty(\"pipelineId\").GetString()!;\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192            var kpiPayload = JsonSerializer.SerializeToElement(new\n   291\u2192            {\n   292\u2192                pipelineId,\n   293\u2192                leadTimeMinutes,\n   294\u2192                dequeuedDate = deployedAt.Date\n   295\u2192            });\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192            return Task.FromResult(new KpiRecord(\"deployment_lead_time\", kpiPayload));\n   298\u2192        }\n   299\u2192    }\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192    #endregion\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192    #region === Dead Letter Queue Components ==========================================================================\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192    /// <summary>\n   306\u2192    /// Simple abstraction for publishing dead-letter messages.\n   307\u2192    /// </summary>\n   308\u2192    public interface IDlqPublisher\n   309\u2192    {\n   310\u2192        Task PublishAsync(DlqMessage message, CancellationToken token);\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192    /// <summary>\n   314\u2192    /// SNS-backed DLQ publisher used in production.  Topic ARN is provided via environment variable.\n   315\u2192    /// </summary>\n   316\u2192    internal sealed class SnsDlqPublisher : IDlqPublisher\n   317\u2192    {\n   318\u2192        private static readonly string TopicArn =\n   319\u2192            Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\"DLQ_TOPIC_ARN\")\n   320\u2192            ?? throw new InvalidOperationException(\"DLQ_TOPIC_ARN environment variable not set.\");\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192        private readonly IAmazonSimpleNotificationService _snsClient;\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192        public SnsDlqPublisher() : this(new AmazonSimpleNotificationServiceClient()) { }\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192        // ctor injection facilitates unit testing with a mocked IAmazonSimpleNotificationService.\n   327\u2192        public SnsDlqPublisher(IAmazonSimpleNotificationService snsClient)\n   328\u2192        {\n   329\u2192            _snsClient = snsClient;\n   330\u2192        }\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192        public async Task PublishAsync(DlqMessage message, CancellationToken token)\n   333\u2192        {\n   334\u2192            var request = new PublishRequest\n   335\u2192            {\n   336\u2192                TopicArn = TopicArn,\n   337\u2192                Message = JsonSerializer.Serialize(message)\n   338\u2192            };\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192            await _snsClient.PublishAsync(request, token).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   341\u2192        }\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192    /// <summary>\n   345\u2192    /// Canonical schema for dead-letter messages.\n   346\u2192    /// </summary>\n   347\u2192    public sealed record DlqMessage(\n   348\u2192        string Source,\n   349\u2192        string Reason,\n   350\u2192        object OriginalEvent)\n   351\u2192    {\n   352\u2192        public static DlqMessage FromDeadLetter(KinesisEvent.KinesisEventRecord record, Exception ex) =>\n   353\u2192            new(\n   354\u2192                Source: \"StreamTransformationCoordinator\",\n   355\u2192                Reason: ex.ToString(),\n   356\u2192                OriginalEvent: new\n   357\u2192                {\n   358\u2192                    record.EventId,\n   359\u2192                    PartitionKey = record.Kinesis.PartitionKey,\n   360\u2192                    SequenceNumber = record.Kinesis.SequenceNumber\n   361\u2192                });\n   362\u2192    }\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192    #endregion\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192    #region === Data Contracts ========================================================================================\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192    /// <summary>\n   369\u2192    /// Lightweight representation of a raw telemetry event pulled from Kinesis.\n   370\u2192    /// </summary>\n   371\u2192    /// <param name=\"ContentType\">MIME-type identifying the event schema.</param>\n   372\u2192    /// <param name=\"Payload\">JSON payload of the event.</param>\n   373\u2192    /// <param name=\"Timestamp\">Event-time.</param>\n   374\u2192    public sealed record RawEvent(\n   375\u2192        string ContentType,\n   376\u2192        JsonElement Payload,\n   377\u2192        DateTime Timestamp);\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192    /// <summary>\n   380\u2192    /// Normalized KPI record emitted by a transformation strategy.\n   381\u2192    /// </summary>\n   382\u2192    /// <param name=\"KpiName\">Short identifier of the KPI.</param>\n   383\u2192    /// <param name=\"DerivedData\">JSON payload containing computed KPI fields.</param>\n   384\u2192    public sealed record KpiRecord(\n   385\u2192        string KpiName,\n   386\u2192        JsonElement DerivedData);\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192    #endregion\n   389\u2192}\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192#pragma warning restore CA1031\n   392\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
99. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_10.txt"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```csharp\n     2\u2192// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n     3\u2192//  Project : PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n     4\u2192//  File    : module_10.txt   (implementation only; filename is controlled by build pipeline)\n     5\u2192//  License : MIT\n     6\u2192// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n     7\u2192//  Description\n     8\u2192//  ------------\n     9\u2192//  Transformation module that materialises productivity-oriented KPIs from a heterogenous event feed.\n    10\u2192//  Implements the Strategy-Pattern so that additional KPIs can be plugged in without redeploying the\n    11\u2192//  compute units.  The module is designed to execute inside an AWS Lambda but is consciously decoupled\n    12\u2192//  from the hosting environment (no direct dependency on Amazon.Lambda.* packages) so it can also be\n    13\u2192//  reused by Glue, Fargate, or on-prem batch jobs.\n    14\u2192// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192using System;\n    17\u2192using System.Collections.Concurrent;\n    18\u2192using System.Collections.Generic;\n    19\u2192using System.Diagnostics;\n    20\u2192using System.Linq;\n    21\u2192using System.Threading;\n    22\u2192using System.Threading.Tasks;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192using Amazon.S3;\n    25\u2192using Amazon.S3.Model;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192namespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Transformations\n    30\u2192{\n    31\u2192    #region Public DTOs / Contracts -----------------------------------------------------------------\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192    /// <summary>\n    34\u2192    /// Thin wrapper around any raw event that enters the warehouse.\n    35\u2192    /// Envelopes allow us to standardise metadata required for transformation,\n    36\u2192    /// without rewriting every single ingestion lambda when an attribute is added later on.\n    37\u2192    /// </summary>\n    38\u2192    /// <remarks>\n    39\u2192    /// The schema mirrors the one emitted by the <c>EventIngressProcessor</c> lambda.\n    40\u2192    /// </remarks>\n    41\u2192    public sealed record EventEnvelope\n    42\u2192    {\n    43\u2192        public required string EventId          { get; init; }\n    44\u2192        public required string EventType        { get; init; }\n    45\u2192        public required DateTimeOffset Created  { get; init; }\n    46\u2192        public required IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string?> Metadata { get; init; }\n    47\u2192        public required string PayloadJson      { get; init; }   // Raw JSON blob \u2013 don\u2019t parse unless strictly needed.\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    /// <summary>\n    51\u2192    /// Normalised, strongly-typed KPI result produced by one of the strategy implementations.\n    52\u2192    /// </summary>\n    53\u2192    public sealed record KpiCalculationResult\n    54\u2192    {\n    55\u2192        public required string              KpiName         { get; init; }\n    56\u2192        public required DateTimeOffset      WindowStartUtc  { get; init; }\n    57\u2192        public required DateTimeOffset      WindowEndUtc    { get; init; }\n    58\u2192        public required decimal             Value           { get; init; }\n    59\u2192        public required IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> Dimensions      { get; init; }\n    60\u2192        public          IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? AdditionalMeta { get; init; }\n    61\u2192    }\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    /// <summary>\n    64\u2192    /// Contract for a pluggable KPI strategy.\n    65\u2192    /// </summary>\n    66\u2192    public interface IKpiCalculationStrategy\n    67\u2192    {\n    68\u2192        /// <summary>Name of the KPI \u2013 MUST be unique.</summary>\n    69\u2192        string KpiName { get; }\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192        /// <summary>Returns <c>true</c> if the strategy can handle the given event.</summary>\n    72\u2192        bool CanHandle(EventEnvelope envelope);\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192        /// <summary>\n    75\u2192        /// Performs calculation and yields one or more KPI rows.  Strategies may buffer events\n    76\u2192        /// internally if the KPI spans a sliding window (e.g., Deployment Lead-Time).\n    77\u2192        /// </summary>\n    78\u2192        ValueTask<IEnumerable<KpiCalculationResult>> ProcessAsync(\n    79\u2192            EventEnvelope envelope,\n    80\u2192            CancellationToken                        ct = default);\n    81\u2192    }\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192    #endregion\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    #region Transformation Engine -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    /// <summary>\n    88\u2192    /// Coordinates incoming event stream and dispatches to registered KPI strategies.\n    89\u2192    /// Takes care of parallelism, back-pressure, exception isolation, and durability (S3 write).\n    90\u2192    /// </summary>\n    91\u2192    public sealed class KpiTransformationEngine : IAsyncDisposable\n    92\u2192    {\n    93\u2192        private readonly IReadOnlyList<IKpiCalculationStrategy> _strategies;\n    94\u2192        private readonly IS3KpiWriter                           _writer;\n    95\u2192        private readonly ILogger<KpiTransformationEngine>       _logger;\n    96\u2192        private readonly SemaphoreSlim                          _parallelism;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192        public KpiTransformationEngine(\n    99\u2192            IEnumerable<IKpiCalculationStrategy> strategies,\n   100\u2192            IS3KpiWriter                           writer,\n   101\u2192            ILogger<KpiTransformationEngine>       logger,\n   102\u2192            int                                    maxDegreeOfParallelism = 8)\n   103\u2192        {\n   104\u2192            _strategies   = strategies?.ToList() ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(strategies));\n   105\u2192            _writer       = writer     ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(writer));\n   106\u2192            _logger       = logger     ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(logger));\n   107\u2192            if (!_strategies.Any())\n   108\u2192                throw new ArgumentException(\"At least one KPI strategy must be provided.\", nameof(strategies));\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192            _parallelism  = new SemaphoreSlim(maxDegreeOfParallelism, maxDegreeOfParallelism);\n   111\u2192        }\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192        /// <summary>\n   114\u2192        /// Orchestrates transformation for a batch of events.\n   115\u2192        /// </summary>\n   116\u2192        public async ValueTask ExecuteAsync(\n   117\u2192            IEnumerable<EventEnvelope> events,\n   118\u2192            CancellationToken         ct = default)\n   119\u2192        {\n   120\u2192            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(events);\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192            var tasks = new List<Task>();\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192            foreach (var envelope in events)\n   125\u2192            {\n   126\u2192                await _parallelism.WaitAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192                // Fire-and-forget \u2013 safe because we await all tasks in the list below\n   129\u2192                tasks.Add(Task.Run(async () =>\n   130\u2192                {\n   131\u2192                    try\n   132\u2192                    {\n   133\u2192                        await DispatchToStrategiesAsync(envelope, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   134\u2192                    }\n   135\u2192                    catch (Exception ex)\n   136\u2192                    {\n   137\u2192                        _logger.LogError(ex,\n   138\u2192                            \"Uncaught exception while processing event {EventId}. \" +\n   139\u2192                            \"The event will be re-queued to the DLQ by the Lambda runtime.\",\n   140\u2192                            envelope.EventId);\n   141\u2192                        // Bubble up to trigger Lambda DLQ \u2013 do NOT swallow\n   142\u2192                        throw;\n   143\u2192                    }\n   144\u2192                    finally\n   145\u2192                    {\n   146\u2192                        _parallelism.Release();\n   147\u2192                    }\n   148\u2192                }, ct));\n   149\u2192            }\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192            await Task.WhenAll(tasks).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   152\u2192        }\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192        private async Task DispatchToStrategiesAsync(EventEnvelope envelope, CancellationToken ct)\n   155\u2192        {\n   156\u2192            var sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192            var interestedStrategies = _strategies.Where(s => s.CanHandle(envelope)).ToArray();\n   159\u2192            if (interestedStrategies.Length == 0)\n   160\u2192            {\n   161\u2192                _logger.LogDebug(\"No KPI strategy registered for event {EventType}.\", envelope.EventType);\n   162\u2192                return;\n   163\u2192            }\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192            var kpiRows = new ConcurrentBag<KpiCalculationResult>();\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192            // Parallel but limited to #strategies so trivial overhead\n   168\u2192            var strategyTasks = interestedStrategies.Select(async strategy =>\n   169\u2192            {\n   170\u2192                try\n   171\u2192                {\n   172\u2192                    var rows = await strategy.ProcessAsync(envelope, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   173\u2192                    foreach (var row in rows) kpiRows.Add(row);\n   174\u2192                }\n   175\u2192                catch (Exception ex)\n   176\u2192                {\n   177\u2192                    _logger.LogWarning(ex,\n   178\u2192                        \"Strategy {KpiStrategy} failed while processing EventId={EventId}. Skipping strategy.\",\n   179\u2192                        strategy.KpiName, envelope.EventId);\n   180\u2192                }\n   181\u2192            });\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192            await Task.WhenAll(strategyTasks).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192            // Commit results to the Data Lake (S3, parquet files partitioned by YYYY/MM/DD)\n   186\u2192            if (kpiRows.Count > 0)\n   187\u2192            {\n   188\u2192                await _writer.WriteAsync(kpiRows, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   189\u2192            }\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192            _logger.LogInformation(\n   192\u2192                \"Processed EventId={EventId} in {ElapsedMs:n0} ms with {RowCount} KPI rows.\",\n   193\u2192                envelope.EventId, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds, kpiRows.Count);\n   194\u2192        }\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192        public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()\n   197\u2192        {\n   198\u2192            _parallelism?.Dispose();\n   199\u2192            if (_writer is IAsyncDisposable asyncDisposable)\n   200\u2192                await asyncDisposable.DisposeAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);\n   201\u2192        }\n   202\u2192    }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192    #endregion\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192    #region Strategy Implementations ----------------------------------------------------------------\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192    /// <summary>\n   209\u2192    /// Calculates the ratio between focus time and total working hours on a per-user, per-day basis.\n   210\u2192    /// Source events:\n   211\u2192    ///   - CalendarEventCreated / Updated\n   212\u2192    ///   - WorkSessionStarted / Ended (emitted by IDE plugins, CLI wrappers, browser extensions, \u2026)\n   213\u2192    /// </summary>\n   214\u2192    internal sealed class FocusTimeRatioStrategy : IKpiCalculationStrategy\n   215\u2192    {\n   216\u2192        private const string CalendarEventType = \"CalendarEvent\";\n   217\u2192        private const string WorkSessionType   = \"WorkSession\";\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192        public string KpiName => \"focus_time_ratio\";\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192        public bool CanHandle(EventEnvelope envelope)\n   222\u2192            => envelope.EventType is CalendarEventType or WorkSessionType;\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192        public async ValueTask<IEnumerable<KpiCalculationResult>> ProcessAsync(\n   225\u2192            EventEnvelope   envelope,\n   226\u2192            CancellationToken ct = default)\n   227\u2192        {\n   228\u2192            // NOTE: In a real-world scenario, we would use a distributed cache (e.g., DAX or Redis)\n   229\u2192            //       to store partial aggregates.  For brevity, this implementation simply returns\n   230\u2192            //       an immediate KPI row when sufficient information is present on the event itself.\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192            if (envelope.EventType == WorkSessionType &&\n   233\u2192                envelope.Metadata.TryGetValue(\"DurationMinutes\", out var durationStr) &&\n   234\u2192                int.TryParse(durationStr, out var durationMinutes))\n   235\u2192            {\n   236\u2192                var userId = envelope.Metadata[\"UserId\"]!;\n   237\u2192                var date   = envelope.Created.UtcDateTime.Date;\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192                var result = new KpiCalculationResult\n   240\u2192                {\n   241\u2192                    KpiName        = KpiName,\n   242\u2192                    WindowStartUtc = date,\n   243\u2192                    WindowEndUtc   = date.AddDays(1),\n   244\u2192                    Value          = durationMinutes, // later aggregated to obtain total focus time\n   245\u2192                    Dimensions = new Dictionary<string, string>\n   246\u2192                    {\n   247\u2192                        [\"user_id\"] = userId,\n   248\u2192                        [\"metric\"]  = \"focus_minutes\"\n   249\u2192                    }\n   250\u2192                };\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192                return new[] { result };\n   253\u2192            }\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192            if (envelope.EventType == CalendarEventType &&\n   256\u2192                envelope.Metadata.TryGetValue(\"DurationMinutes\", out var calendarDurationStr) &&\n   257\u2192                int.TryParse(calendarDurationStr, out var meetingMinutes))\n   258\u2192            {\n   259\u2192                var userId = envelope.Metadata[\"OrganizerId\"]!;\n   260\u2192                var date   = envelope.Created.UtcDateTime.Date;\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192                var result = new KpiCalculationResult\n   263\u2192                {\n   264\u2192                    KpiName        = KpiName,\n   265\u2192                    WindowStartUtc = date,\n   266\u2192                    WindowEndUtc   = date.AddDays(1),\n   267\u2192                    Value          = meetingMinutes,\n   268\u2192                    Dimensions = new Dictionary<string, string>\n   269\u2192                    {\n   270\u2192                        [\"user_id\"] = userId,\n   271\u2192                        [\"metric\"]  = \"meeting_minutes\"\n   272\u2192                    }\n   273\u2192                };\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192                return new[] { result };\n   276\u2192            }\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192            return Array.Empty<KpiCalculationResult>();\n   279\u2192        }\n   280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192    /// <summary>\n   283\u2192    /// Computes deployment lead-time (commit \u2192 production) on a per-repo basis.\n   284\u2192    /// Maintains a small in-memory buffer which is flushed every <see cref=\"FlushInterval\"/>.\n   285\u2192    /// </summary>\n   286\u2192    internal sealed class DeploymentLeadTimeStrategy : IKpiCalculationStrategy, IDisposable\n   287\u2192    {\n   288\u2192        private const string CommitEventType     = \"CodeCommitPushed\";\n   289\u2192        private const string ProdDeployEventType = \"CdPipelineDeployed\";\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192        private readonly TimeSpan                               FlushInterval     = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5);\n   292\u2192        private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, DateTime> _commitTimestamps = new(StringComparer.Ordinal);\n   293\u2192        private readonly Timer                                  _flushTimer;\n   294\u2192        private readonly ILogger                                _logger;\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192        public string KpiName => \"deployment_lead_time\";\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192        public DeploymentLeadTimeStrategy(ILogger<DeploymentLeadTimeStrategy> logger)\n   299\u2192        {\n   300\u2192            _logger      = logger;\n   301\u2192            _flushTimer  = new Timer(_ => FlushExpired(), null, FlushInterval, FlushInterval);\n   302\u2192        }\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192        public bool CanHandle(EventEnvelope envelope)\n   305\u2192            => envelope.EventType is CommitEventType or ProdDeployEventType;\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192        public async ValueTask<IEnumerable<KpiCalculationResult>> ProcessAsync(\n   308\u2192            EventEnvelope envelope,\n   309\u2192            CancellationToken ct = default)\n   310\u2192        {\n   311\u2192            switch (envelope.EventType)\n   312\u2192            {\n   313\u2192                case CommitEventType:\n   314\u2192                    {\n   315\u2192                        var commitSha  = envelope.Metadata[\"CommitSha\"]!;\n   316\u2192                        _commitTimestamps[commitSha] = envelope.Created.UtcDateTime;\n   317\u2192                        break;\n   318\u2192                    }\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192                case ProdDeployEventType:\n   321\u2192                    {\n   322\u2192                        var commitSha = envelope.Metadata[\"CommitSha\"]!;\n   323\u2192                        if (_commitTimestamps.TryRemove(commitSha, out var commitTime))\n   324\u2192                        {\n   325\u2192                            var leadTimeMinutes = (envelope.Created.UtcDateTime - commitTime).TotalMinutes;\n   326\u2192                            var repo            = envelope.Metadata[\"Repository\"]!;\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192                            var kpiRow = new KpiCalculationResult\n   329\u2192                            {\n   330\u2192                                KpiName        = KpiName,\n   331\u2192                                WindowStartUtc = commitTime,\n   332\u2192                                WindowEndUtc   = envelope.Created.UtcDateTime,\n   333\u2192                                Value          = (decimal)leadTimeMinutes,\n   334\u2192                                Dimensions = new Dictionary<string, string>\n   335\u2192                                {\n   336\u2192                                    [\"repository\"] = repo\n   337\u2192                                }\n   338\u2192                            };\n   339\u2192                            return new[] { kpiRow };\n   340\u2192                        }\n   341\u2192                        break;\n   342\u2192                    }\n   343\u2192            }\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192            return Array.Empty<KpiCalculationResult>();\n   346\u2192        }\n   347\u2192\n   348\u2192        private void FlushExpired()\n   349\u2192        {\n   350\u2192            var threshold = DateTime.UtcNow.AddHours(-24); // assume deploy happens within 24 h\n   351\u2192            foreach (var kvp in _commitTimestamps)\n   352\u2192            {\n   353\u2192                if (kvp.Value < threshold && _commitTimestamps.TryRemove(kvp.Key, out _))\n   354\u2192                {\n   355\u2192                    _logger.LogDebug(\n   356\u2192                        \"Dropped stale commit {CommitSha} for deployment lead-time KPI (older than 24h).\",\n   357\u2192                        kvp.Key);\n   358\u2192                }\n   359\u2192            }\n   360\u2192        }\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192        public void Dispose()\n   363\u2192        {\n   364\u2192            _flushTimer.Dispose();\n   365\u2192        }\n   366\u2192    }\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192    #endregion\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192    #region Persistence Layer -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192    /// <summary>\n   373\u2192    /// Simple abstraction that serialises KPI rows as ND-JSON and stores them in S3.\n   374\u2192    /// Real-world implementation could write Apache Parquet or Iceberg; ND-JSON keeps demo lightweight.\n   375\u2192    /// </summary>\n   376\u2192    public interface IS3KpiWriter : IAsyncDisposable\n   377\u2192    {\n   378\u2192        ValueTask WriteAsync(\n   379\u2192            IEnumerable<KpiCalculationResult> rows,\n   380\u2192            CancellationToken                 ct = default);\n   381\u2192    }\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192    /// <summary>\n   384\u2192    /// Production-ready S3 writer implementation with retry/back-off and partitioned key structure.\n   385\u2192    /// </summary>\n   386\u2192    internal sealed class S3KpiWriter : IS3KpiWriter\n   387\u2192    {\n   388\u2192        private readonly IAmazonS3                 _s3;\n   389\u2192        private readonly string                    _bucket;\n   390\u2192        private readonly ILogger<S3KpiWriter>      _logger;\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192        private static readonly AmazonS3RetryPolicy RetryPolicy = new();\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192        public S3KpiWriter(IAmazonS3 s3, string bucket, ILogger<S3KpiWriter> logger)\n   395\u2192        {\n   396\u2192            _s3     = s3     ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(s3));\n   397\u2192            _bucket = bucket ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(bucket));\n   398\u2192            _logger = logger ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(logger));\n   399\u2192        }\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192        public async ValueTask WriteAsync(\n   402\u2192            IEnumerable<KpiCalculationResult> rows,\n   403\u2192            CancellationToken                 ct = default)\n   404\u2192        {\n   405\u2192            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(rows);\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192            var nowUtc     = DateTime.UtcNow;\n   408\u2192            var partition  = $\"{nowUtc:yyyy/MM/dd}\";\n   409\u2192            var objectKey  = $\"kpi/partition_date={partition}/{Guid.NewGuid():N}.jsonl\";\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192            // Serialise as ND-JSON\n   412\u2192            var jsonLines  = string.Join('\\n', rows.Select(row => System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(row)));\n   413\u2192\n   414\u2192            var request = new PutObjectRequest\n   415\u2192            {\n   416\u2192                BucketName  = _bucket,\n   417\u2192                Key         = objectKey,\n   418\u2192                ContentBody = jsonLines,\n   419\u2192                ContentType = \"application/json\"\n   420\u2192            };\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192            await RetryPolicy.ExecuteAsync(\n   423\u2192                async () => await _s3.PutObjectAsync(request, ct).ConfigureAwait(false),\n   424\u2192                ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   425\u2192\n   426\u2192            _logger.LogDebug(\"Wrote {RowCount} KPI rows to s3://{Bucket}/{Key}.\", rows.Count(), _bucket, objectKey);\n   427\u2192        }\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192        public ValueTask DisposeAsync() => ValueTask.CompletedTask;\n   430\u2192    }\n   431\u2192\n   432\u2192    /// <summary>\n   433\u2192    /// Declarative, exponential back-off retry wrapper for S3 operations.\n   434\u2192    /// </summary>\n   435\u2192    internal sealed class AmazonS3RetryPolicy\n   436\u2192    {\n   437\u2192        private static readonly TimeSpan[] BackOffSchedule =\n   438\u2192        {\n   439\u2192            TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(100),\n   440\u2192            TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250),\n   441\u2192            TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500),\n   442\u2192            TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1),\n   443\u2192            TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2)\n   444\u2192        };\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192        public async Task ExecuteAsync(Func<Task> operation, CancellationToken ct)\n   447\u2192        {\n   448\u2192            var attempt = 0;\n   449\u2192            Exception? lastError = null;\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192            foreach (var delay in BackOffSchedule.Append(TimeSpan.Zero))\n   452\u2192            {\n   453\u2192                try\n   454\u2192                {\n   455\u2192                    ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();\n   456\u2192                    await operation().ConfigureAwait(false);\n   457\u2192                    return; // success\n   458\u2192                }\n   459\u2192                catch (Exception ex) when (IsTransient(ex))\n   460\u2192                {\n   461\u2192                    lastError = ex;\n   462\u2192                    attempt++;\n   463\u2192                    if (delay > TimeSpan.Zero)\n   464\u2192                        await Task.Delay(delay, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);\n   465\u2192                }\n   466\u2192            }\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192            throw new InvalidOperationException(\n   469\u2192                $\"S3 operation failed after {attempt} retries.\", lastError);\n   470\u2192        }\n   471\u2192\n   472\u2192        private static bool IsTransient(Exception ex)\n   473\u2192        {\n   474\u2192            // TODO: Add richer fault-analysis; for now assume all AmazonS3Exception with 5xx are transient.\n   475\u2192            return ex is AmazonS3Exception s3Ex && (int)s3Ex.StatusCode >= 500;\n   476\u2192        }\n   477\u2192    }\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192    #endregion\n   480\u2192}\n   481\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
