"=== module_35.txt ===\n    /// Publishes failed records to an AWS SQS DLQ.\n    /// </summary>\n    internal sealed class SqsDeadLetterPublisher : IDisposable\n    {\n        private readonly AmazonSQSClient _client;\n        private readonly string _queueUrl;\n        private readonly ILogger<SqsDeadLetterPublisher> _logger;\n\n        public SqsDeadLetterPublisher(string queueUrl, ILogger<SqsDeadLetterPublisher> logger)\n        {\n            _queueUrl = queueUrl ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(queueUrl));\n            _logger   = logger   ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(logger));\n\n            // Use default credential chain (Lambda, ECS Task, etc.)\n            _client = new AmazonSQSClient();\n        }\n--\n                    Error = errorMessage\n                });\n\n                await _client.SendMessageAsync(new SendMessageRequest\n                {\n                    QueueUrl    = _queueUrl,\n                    MessageBody = body\n                }, token);\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n=== module_61.txt ===\n    {\n        private readonly KpiTransformationRegistry _registry;\n        private readonly IServiceProvider _serviceProvider;\n        private readonly ILogger<KpiTransformationEngine> _logger;\n        private readonly IAmazonSQS? _sqs; // Optional DLQ sink.\n        private readonly string? _deadLetterQueueUrl;\n        private readonly int _degreeOfParallelism;\n\n        public KpiTransformationEngine(\n            KpiTransformationRegistry registry,\n            IServiceProvider serviceProvider,\n--\n                yield return enumerator.Current;\n                await Task.Yield(); // Yield to avoid hogging thread in CPU-bound loops.\n            }\n        }\n\n        private async Task SendToDeadLetterQueueAsync(\n            IEnumerable<RawEvent> events,\n            Exception ex,\n            CancellationToken token)\n        {\n            if (_sqs is null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_deadLetterQueueUrl)) return;\n=== module_72.txt ===\n    public sealed class FireAndForgetDispatcher : ITransformedEventDispatcher, IDisposable\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger _log;\n        private readonly IAmazonKinesis _kinesis;\n        private readonly string _targetStreamName;\n        private readonly ConcurrentQueue<Amazon.Kinesis.Model.PutRecordsRequestEntry> _buffer = new();\n\n        public FireAndForgetDispatcher(ILogger log)\n        {\n            _log              = log;\n            _kinesis          = new AmazonKinesisClient(); // region inferred from Lambda environment\n=== module_75.txt ===\n        private readonly Task _backgroundTask;\n\n        // Internal buffer storing events inside the current active window(s)\n        // We deliberately use a ConcurrentQueue to keep insertion O(1) and avoid\n        // global locks on the hot path.\n        private readonly ConcurrentQueue<ProductivityEvent> _buffer = new();\n\n        // Latest watermark processed (monotonic)\n        private DateTimeOffset _currentWindowCutoff;\n\n        public TimeSeriesWindowAggregator("
110. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/README.md"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```markdown\n     2\u2192# PulseOps Warehouse \u2014 Serverless Productivity Intelligence Fabric\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192![Build](https://github.com/pulseops/warehouse/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)\n     5\u2192![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/pulseops/warehouse.svg)\n     6\u2192![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/pulseops/warehouse)\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192PulseOps Warehouse is an expert-grade, **serverless** data-warehouse solution that empowers enterprises to quantify and improve knowledge-worker productivity\u2014without touching a single server.  \n     9\u2192Built with **C# /.NET 7**, **AWS CDK**, and 100 % managed AWS services, the platform ingests telemetry from calendars, SCMs, CI/CD systems, and SaaS apps, then distills that fire-hose into actionable KPIs such as:\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192* Focus-Time Ratio\n    12\u2192* Deployment Lead-Time\n    13\u2192* Meeting Cost Index\n    14\u2192* MTTR / MTTD\n    15\u2192* Cycle-Time & Flow-Efficiency\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192---\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192## \u2728 Key Capabilities\n    20\u2192| Category              | Implementation Highlights                                                                                                                                                   |\n    21\u2192|-----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n    22\u2192| Data Storage          | Amazon S3 Data Lake, Amazon Athena / Glue Data Catalog, Lake-Formation fine-grained permissions                                                                              |\n    23\u2192| Stream Processing     | Kinesis Data Streams + Lambda (GoF Event-Streaming & Observer pattern)                                                                                                       |\n    24\u2192| Batch Processing      | AWS Glue, Lambda-powered Step Functions, configurable back-fill scheduler                                                                                                    |\n    25\u2192| Parallel Processing   | Fan-out via Kinesis shards, per-partition Lambda concurrency, S3 Select push-down                                                                                           |\n    26\u2192| Transformation Logic  | Strategy pattern with hot-swappable transformation DLLs registered in S3                                                                                                    |\n    27\u2192| Infrastructure-as-Code| AWS CDK (C#) stacks, GitHub Actions CI/CD, multi-account deployment pipelines                                                                                                |\n    28\u2192| Observability         | Structured JSON logging, CloudWatch Metrics, DLQ fan-out & PagerDuty auto-escalation                                                                                        |\n    29\u2192| Security              | IAM least-privilege, KMS encryption, OIDC GitHub Actions roles, S3 Block Public Access                                                                                      |\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192---\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192## \u2699\ufe0f Architecture Overview\n    34\u2192```mermaid\n    35\u2192flowchart LR\n    36\u2192    subgraph Ingestion\n    37\u2192        A[Calendar Webhook Lambda]\n    38\u2192        B[GitHub Webhook Lambda]\n    39\u2192        C[CI/CD Logs Firehose]\n    40\u2192        D[SaaS Telemetry Streams]\n    41\u2192    end\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    subgraph Streaming Fabric\n    44\u2192        Kinesis[(Kinesis Data Stream)]\n    45\u2192        E[Stream Processor Lambda]\n    46\u2192    end\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192    subgraph Batch Fabric\n    49\u2192        G[AWS Glue ETL Job]\n    50\u2192        H[Step Functions Back-fill]\n    51\u2192    end\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192    subgraph Storage\n    54\u2192        S3Lake[(S3 Data Lake)]\n    55\u2192        GlueCatalog[(Glue Data Catalog)]\n    56\u2192        Athena[(Athena / Viewer)]\n    57\u2192    end\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    subgraph Dashboards\n    60\u2192        Q[QuickSight\\nWorkspace]\n    61\u2192    end\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    A -->|JSON| Kinesis\n    64\u2192    B -->|Avro| Kinesis\n    65\u2192    C -->|Parquet| Kinesis\n    66\u2192    D -->|JSON| Kinesis\n    67\u2192    Kinesis --> E\n    68\u2192    E -->|Clean Parquet| S3Lake\n    69\u2192    G --> S3Lake\n    70\u2192    H --> G\n    71\u2192    S3Lake --> GlueCatalog --> Athena --> Q\n    72\u2192```\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192---\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192## \ud83d\uddc2\ufe0f Folder Layout\n    77\u2192```\n    78\u2192data-warehouse/\n    79\u2192\u251c\u2500\u2500 cdk/                     # AWS CDK stacks (Infra-as-Code)\n    80\u2192\u251c\u2500\u2500 src/\n    81\u2192\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PulseOps.Common/     # Shared domain & utility classes\n    82\u2192\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PulseOps.Transform/  # Strategy-pattern transformations\n    83\u2192\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PulseOps.Stream/     # Kinesis event consumers\n    84\u2192\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 PulseOps.Batch/      # Glue jobs & batch orchestrations\n    85\u2192\u251c\u2500\u2500 tests/                   # xUnit integration & unit tests\n    86\u2192\u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md\n    87\u2192```\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192---\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192## \ud83d\ude80 Quick Start (Developer)\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192> Prerequisites: .NET 7 SDK, Node.js 18, AWS CLI, Docker (\ud83d\udcc8 local stack)\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192```bash\n    96\u2192git clone https://github.com/pulseops/warehouse.git\n    97\u2192cd warehouse\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192# Bootstrap CDK (one-time per account / region)\n   100\u2192./scripts/bootstrap.sh\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192# Synthesize CloudFormation templates\n   103\u2192dotnet build ./cdk\n   104\u2192cdk synth\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192# Deploy dev stack to your AWS dev account\n   107\u2192export PULSE_ENV=dev\n   108\u2192cdk deploy --all\n   109\u2192```\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192### Streaming locally with Kinesis MSK Lite\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192```bash\n   114\u2192docker compose up -d local-kinesis\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192dotnet run --project src/PulseOps.Stream/ \\\n   117\u2192  --Environment=local \\\n   118\u2192  --KinesisEndpoint=http://localhost:4567\n   119\u2192```\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192### Execute Glue Job locally\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192```bash\n   124\u2192docker run --rm \\\n   125\u2192  -v \"$PWD\":/workspace \\\n   126\u2192  amazon/aws-glue-libs:glue_libs_4.0.0_image_01 \\\n   127\u2192  spark-submit /workspace/src/PulseOps.Batch/GlueJob.py --DEV\n   128\u2192```\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192---\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192## \ud83e\udde9 Extending Transformation Logic\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192PulseOps applies the Strategy pattern so that domain teams can **drop-in** new business logic without redeploying the entire mesh.\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192```csharp\n   137\u2192public sealed class FocusTimeTransformation : ITransformationStrategy\n   138\u2192{\n   139\u2192    public string Name => \"focus-time\";\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192    public Task<IEnumerable<KpiRecord>> ExecuteAsync(RawEvent @event, CancellationToken ct = default)\n   142\u2192    {\n   143\u2192        if (@event.Type is not \"calendar\")\n   144\u2192            return Task.FromResult(Enumerable.Empty<KpiRecord>());\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192        var duration = @event.Payload![\"durationSeconds\"].GetInt32();\n   147\u2192        var isFocus = !@event.Payload[\"isRecurring\"].GetBoolean() &&\n   148\u2192                      @event.Payload[\"attendees\"].GetArrayLength() == 1;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192        var kpi = new KpiRecord(\n   151\u2192            Key: $\"focus:{@event.UserId}\",\n   152\u2192            Value: isFocus ? duration : 0,\n   153\u2192            Timestamp: @event.OccurredAt);\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192        return Task.FromResult(new[] { kpi }.AsEnumerable());\n   156\u2192    }\n   157\u2192}\n   158\u2192```\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192Deploy:\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192```bash\n   163\u2192dotnet publish src/YourCustom.Transform/ -o ./artifacts\n   164\u2192aws s3 cp ./artifacts s3://pulseops-transformations/focus-time/\n   165\u2192```\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192The Stream Processor Lambda looks up the latest DLL from S3 on cold-start, loads it via `AssemblyLoadContext`, and starts routing events to your new strategy\u2014**no redeploy required**.\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192---\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192## \ud83d\udee1\ufe0f Security & Compliance\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192* All data at rest is encrypted with **AWS KMS CMKs**\n   174\u2192* Data Lake buckets enforce **S3 Block Public Access**\n   175\u2192* **Lake Formation** governs cross-team access with fine-grained column-level permissions\n   176\u2192* **IAM Identity Center** enables SSO & integrates with Okta/AAD\n   177\u2192* Audit via **AWS CloudTrail**, **EventBridge** rules raise anomalies to Security Hub\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192---\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192## \ud83d\udcca Metrics & Observability\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192| Metric                          | Source                         | Alert Threshold     |\n   184\u2192|---------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------|\n   185\u2192| DLQ message age                 | Kinesis DLQ                   | > 5 min avg         |\n   186\u2192| Transformation latency (p95)    | Lambda `transform()`          | > 800 ms            |\n   187\u2192| Glue job failure count          | CloudWatch Alarm              | \u2265 1 in 24 h         |\n   188\u2192| Data freshness (Athena view)    | Back-Fill Step Functions      | Lag > 2 h           |\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192Alarms route through **PagerDuty** using EventBridge Webhook targets.\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192---\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192## \ud83e\uddea Tests\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192```bash\n   197\u2192dotnet test --configuration Release /p:CollectCoverage=true\n   198\u2192```\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192Tests include:\n   201\u2192* 4 000+ unit tests (xUnit)\n   202\u2192* Integration tests with LocalStack & Kinesis MSK Lite\n   203\u2192* Contract tests for Lambda & Step Functions state machines (`state-machine-tester`)\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192---\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192## \ud83d\udcbc Production Deployment\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192Production releases are handled by GitHub Actions:\n   210\u2192\n   211\u21921. PR merge to `main` triggers **semantic-release** to bump the version.\n   212\u21922. `ci.yml` builds, tests, and publishes ML-signed artifacts to the GitHub Registry.\n   213\u21923. `cdk deploy --require-approval never` stacks into the `prod` AWS account via OIDC.\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192Rollback is one click: CDK retains the previous CloudFormation stack revision.\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192---\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192## \ud83d\udcdd Contributing\n   220\u2192\n   221\u21921. Fork the repo & create your branch (`git checkout -b feature/my-kpi`)\n   222\u21922. Write code + tests, follow **Conventional Commits** (`feat: add cycle-time KPI`)\n   223\u21923. Run `dotnet format` before pushing.\n   224\u21924. PR to `main`; the CI must pass & code coverage \u2265 90 %.\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192All contributions require a **DCO sign-off**.\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192---\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192## \ud83d\udcc4 License\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192Apache-2.0 \u00a9 PulseOps Inc.\n   233\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

112. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_65.txt",
  "limit": 160,
  "offset": 305
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    /// <summary>\n    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n    {\n        private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n        public Task WriteAsync(\n            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n        {\n            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n            return Task.CompletedTask;\n        }\n\n        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n    }\n\n    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  Transformation pipeline (Dataflow) \u2014 high throughput, back-pressure aware\n    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    public sealed class EventTransformationPipeline : IAsyncDisposable\n    {\n        private readonly BufferBlock<WorkActivityEvent>                _input;\n        private readonly TransformManyBlock<WorkActivityEvent, MetricDataPoint> _compute;\n        private readonly ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>                  _sink;\n        private readonly ILogger                                       _log = Log.ForContext<EventTransformationPipeline>();\n        private readonly CancellationTokenSource                        _cts = new();\n\n        public EventTransformationPipeline(\n            StrategyRegistry strategyRegistry,\n            IMetricSink      metricSink,\n            int              maxDegreeOfParallelism = 4,\n            int              boundedCapacity        = 1_024)\n        {\n            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(strategyRegistry);\n            ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(metricSink);\n\n            // Configure blocks\n            _input = new BufferBlock<WorkActivityEvent>(\n                new DataflowBlockOptions\n                {\n                    BoundedCapacity = boundedCapacity\n                });\n\n            _compute = new TransformManyBlock<WorkActivityEvent, MetricDataPoint>(\n                async (workEvent) =>\n                {\n                    try\n                    {\n                        if (strategyRegistry.TryResolve(workEvent, out var strategy))\n                        {\n                            return await strategy.ComputeAsync(\n                                workEvent,\n                                _cts.Token);\n                        }\n\n                        _log.Debug(\"No strategy matched event {@Event}\", workEvent);\n                        return Array.Empty<MetricDataPoint>();\n                    }\n                    catch (Exception ex)\n                    {\n                        _log.Error(\n                            ex,\n                            \"Metric computation failed for event {@Event}\",\n                            workEvent);\n                        return Array.Empty<MetricDataPoint>();\n                    }\n                },\n                new ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions\n                {\n                    MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism,\n                    BoundedCapacity        = boundedCapacity,\n                    CancellationToken      = _cts.Token,\n                    EnsureOrdered          = false\n                });\n\n            _sink = new ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>(\n                async (metric) =>\n                {\n                    try\n                    {\n                        await metricSink.WriteAsync(\n                            new[] { metric },\n                            _cts.Token);\n                    }\n                    catch (Exception ex)\n                    {\n                        _log.Error(\n                            ex,\n                            \"Failed to write metric {@Metric}\",\n                            metric);\n                        // In production, failed metrics would be forwarded to a DLQ.\n                    }\n                },\n                new ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions\n                {\n                    MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism,\n                    BoundedCapacity        = boundedCapacity,\n                    CancellationToken      = _cts.Token,\n                    EnsureOrdered          = false\n                });\n\n            // Wire-up blocks\n            _input.LinkTo(_compute,                    new DataflowLinkOptions { PropagateCompletion = true });\n            _compute.LinkTo(_sink,                     new DataflowLinkOptions { PropagateCompletion = true });\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Enqueues a new event for processing.  Respects back-pressure.\n        /// </summary>\n        public async Task SubmitAsync(\n            WorkActivityEvent  workEvent,\n            CancellationToken  cancellationToken = default)\n        {\n            if (!_input.Post(workEvent))\n            {\n                await _input.SendAsync(workEvent, cancellationToken);\n            }\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Signals graceful shutdown and waits until all buffered work completes.\n        /// </summary>\n        public async Task CompleteAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\n        {\n            _input.Complete();\n            try\n            {\n                await _sink.Completion.WaitAsync(cancellationToken);\n            }\n            catch (OperationCanceledException) when (cancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested)\n            {\n                _cts.Cancel();\n                throw;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()\n        {\n            _cts.Cancel();\n            _input.Complete();\n\n            try\n            {\n                await _sink.Completion;\n            }\n            catch\n            {\n                // Swallow any in-flight exceptions on dispose.\n            }\n\n            _cts.Dispose();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  Example bootstrapping (would live in Lambda entrypoint or ASP.NET Startup)",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_65.txt",
    "numLines": 160,
    "startLine": 305,
    "totalLines": 494
  },
  "type": "text"
}

117. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_68.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```csharp\n// File: src/module_68.txt\n// Language: C#\n// Description:\n//   Lambda entry-point that transforms raw telemetry events into higher-level\n//   productivity insights.  Demonstrates Strategy, Observer, and Pipeline\n//   patterns while integrating with AWS managed services for downstream fan-out.\n//\n//   This module is intentionally self-contained so that it can be dropped into\n//   a unit-test project without additional scaffolding.  In the real\n//   code-base, these types live in discrete files/namespaces.\n//\n// Note: Namespace prefixes shortened for brevity.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Collections.Concurrent;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Text;\nusing System.Text.Json;\nusing System.Text.Json.Serialization;\nusing System.Threading;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Amazon.EventBridge;\nusing Amazon.EventBridge.Model;\nusing Amazon.Lambda.Core;\nusing Amazon.Lambda.KinesisEvents;\nusing Amazon.Runtime;\nusing Amazon.SQS;\nusing Amazon.SQS.Model;\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\nusing Polly;\nusing Polly.Retry;\n\n// ReSharper disable MemberCanBePrivate.Global \u2014 File is flattened for demo purposes.\n[assembly: LambdaSerializer(typeof(Amazon.Lambda.Serialization.SystemTextJson.DefaultLambdaJsonSerializer))]\n\nnamespace PulseOps.Warehouse.Lambda.Transform\n{\n    #region Lambda Entry-Point --------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Serverless transformation Lambda \u2014 invoked by a Kinesis Stream containing raw\n    /// telemetry events.  Delegates business logic to pluggable <see cref=\"ITransformationStrategy\"/>s\n    /// discovered at runtime via <see cref=\"TransformationStrategyFactory\"/>.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class TransformationLambda\n    {\n        private readonly ITransformationStrategyFactory _strategyFactory;\n        private readonly ITelemetryDispatcher            _dispatcher;\n        private readonly ILogger<TransformationLambda>   _logger;\n        private readonly AsyncRetryPolicy                _retryPolicy;\n\n        public TransformationLambda()\n            : this(new TransformationStrategyFactory(),\n                   new EventBridgeDispatcher(),\n                   LambdaLoggerWrapper.Create<TransformationLambda>())\n        { }\n\n        // DI-friendly constructor (used in unit tests)\n        public TransformationLambda(ITransformationStrategyFactory strategyFactory,\n                                    ITelemetryDispatcher dispatcher,\n                                    ILogger<TransformationLambda> logger)\n        {\n            _strategyFactory = strategyFactory ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(strategyFactory));\n            _dispatcher      = dispatcher      ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(dispatcher));\n            _logger          = logger          ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(logger));\n\n            // Standard exponential back-off for transient AWS failures.\n            _retryPolicy = Policy\n                .Handle<Exception>(ex => ex is AmazonServiceException or TaskCanceledException)\n                .WaitAndRetryAsync(\n                    retryCount: 3,\n                    sleepDurationProvider: attempt => TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(200 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)),\n                    onRetry: (ex, span, attempt, _) =>\n                        _logger.LogWarning(ex,\n                            \"Transient failure when dispatching insight (attempt {Attempt}). Retrying in {Delay}\u2026\",\n                            attempt, span));\n        }\n\n        #region Handler ---------------------------------------------------------\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Lambda function handler.\n        /// </summary>\n        /// <param name=\"kinesisEvent\">Batch of Kinesis records.</param>\n        /// <param name=\"context\">Lambda context.</param>\n        public async Task HandlerAsync(KinesisEvent kinesisEvent, ILambdaContext context)\n        {\n            _logger.LogInformation(\"Processing batch with {RecordCount} record(s)\u2026\", kinesisEvent?.Records?.Count);\n\n            var dispatchTasks = new List<Task>();\n\n            foreach (var rec in kinesisEvent.Records)\n            {\n                if (TryParseEvent(rec, out var telemetryEvent))\n                {\n                    var strategy = _strategyFactory.GetStrategy(telemetryEvent.EventType);\n                    if (strategy == null)\n                    {\n                        _logger.LogWarning(\"No strategy registered for event type '{EventType}'. Record skipped.\",\n                            telemetryEvent.EventType);\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    try\n                    {\n                        var insights = await strategy.TransformAsync(telemetryEvent, context.CancellationToken)\n                                                     .ConfigureAwait(false);\n\n                        // Fan-out each insight asynchronously with retry.\n                        foreach (var insight in insights)\n                        {\n                            dispatchTasks.Add(\n                                _retryPolicy.ExecuteAsync(ct => _dispatcher.DispatchAsync(insight, ct),\n                                                          context.CancellationToken));\n                        }\n                    }\n                    catch (Exception ex)\n                    {\n                        _logger.LogError(ex,\n                            \"Error while applying strategy '{Strategy}' to eventType '{EventType}'.\",\n                            strategy.GetType().Name,\n                            telemetryEvent.EventType);\n                        await SendToDlqAsync(rec).ConfigureAwait(false);\n                    }\n                }\n                else\n                {\n                    await SendToDlqAsync(rec).ConfigureAwait(false);\n                }\n            }\n\n            await Task.WhenAll(dispatchTasks).ConfigureAwait(false);\n            _logger.LogInformation(\"Batch processing complete.\");\n        }\n\n        #endregion\n\n        #region Helpers ---------------------------------------------------------\n\n        private bool TryParseEvent(KinesisEvent.KinesisEventRecord record, out TelemetryEvent telemetryEvent)\n        {\n            telemetryEvent = null!;\n            try\n            {\n                var json = Encoding.UTF8.GetString(record.Kinesis.Data.Span);\n                telemetryEvent = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<TelemetryEvent>(json, JsonOptions.Options);\n\n                if (telemetryEvent?.EventType == null)\n                    throw new JsonException(\"Missing mandatory 'eventType' property.\");\n\n                return true;\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n                _logger.LogError(ex, \"Failed to deserialize record into TelemetryEvent.\");\n                return false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        private async Task SendToDlqAsync(KinesisEvent.KinesisEventRecord record)\n        {\n            try\n            {\n                var queueUrl   = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\"DLQ_URL\") ?? string.Empty;\n                var sqsClient  = SqsClientSingleton.Instance;\n\n                // Send raw payload to DLQ for triage.\n                await sqsClient.SendMessageAsync(new SendMessageRequest\n                {\n                    QueueUrl    = queueUrl,\n                    MessageBody = Convert.ToBase64String(record.Kinesis.Data.Span)\n                });\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n                // Log but swallow \u2014 we never want to fail the entire batch because DLQ write failed.\n                _logger.LogError(ex, \"Unable to push faulty record to DLQ.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        #endregion\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region DTOs ---------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    public sealed record TelemetryEvent(\n        [property: JsonPropertyName(\"eventType\")] string EventType,\n        [property: JsonPropertyName(\"timestamp\")] DateTime Timestamp,\n        [property: JsonPropertyName(\"payload\")]   JsonElement Payload);\n\n    public sealed record ProductivityInsight(\n        string InsightType,\n        DateTime ObservedAtUtc,\n        IDictionary<string, object> Metrics);\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Strategy Pattern ---------------------------------------------------\n\n    public interface ITransformationStrategy\n    {\n        string EventType { get; }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Transforms a raw telemetry event into one or more <see cref=\"ProductivityInsight\"/>s.\n        /// </summary>\n        Task<IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityInsight>> TransformAsync(\n            TelemetryEvent telemetryEvent,\n            CancellationToken cancellationToken);\n    }\n\n    public interface ITransformationStrategyFactory\n    {\n        ITransformationStrategy? GetStrategy(string eventType);\n    }\n\n    public sealed class TransformationStrategyFactory : ITransformationStrategyFactory\n    {\n        private static readonly IReadOnlyDictionary<string, Func<ITransformationStrategy>> StrategyMap =\n            new Dictionary<string, Func<ITransformationStrategy>>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            {\n                [\"calendar.focus-time\"]    = () => new FocusTimeStrategy(),\n                [\"deployment.lifecycle\"]   = () => new DeploymentLeadTimeStrategy()\n            };\n\n        private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, ITransformationStrategy> _cache = new();\n\n        public ITransformationStrategy? GetStrategy(string eventType)\n        {\n            return _cache.GetOrAdd(eventType, key =>\n            {\n                return StrategyMap.TryGetValue(key, out var ctor) ? ctor() : null!;\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Concrete Strategies ------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Calculates focus-time ratio from calendar events.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class FocusTimeStrategy : ITransformationStrategy\n    {\n        public string EventType => \"calendar.focus-time\";\n\n        public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityInsight>> TransformAsync(\n            TelemetryEvent telemetryEvent,\n            CancellationToken cancellationToken)\n        {\n            /*\n             * Expected payload:\n             * {\n             *   \"attendee\": \"john@example.com\",\n             *   \"durationMinutes\": 60,\n             *   \"isFocusTime\": true\n             * }\n             */\n\n            var payload = telemetryEvent.Payload;\n            var durationMinutes = payload.GetProperty(\"durationMinutes\").GetInt32();\n            var isFocusTime     = payload.GetProperty(\"isFocusTime\").GetBoolean();\n\n            var metricKey   = isFocusTime ? \"FocusMinutes\" : \"MeetingMinutes\";\n            var metricsDict = new Dictionary<string, object>\n            {\n                [metricKey] = durationMinutes\n            };\n\n            var insight = new ProductivityInsight(\n                InsightType: \"focus-time.ratio\",\n                ObservedAtUtc: telemetryEvent.Timestamp,\n                Metrics: metricsDict);\n\n            return Task.FromResult<IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityInsight>>(new[] { insight });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Calculates deployment lead-time from CI/CD lifecycle events.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class DeploymentLeadTimeStrategy : ITransformationStrategy\n    {\n        public string EventType => \"deployment.lifecycle\";\n\n        public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityInsight>> TransformAsync(\n            TelemetryEvent telemetryEvent,\n            CancellationToken cancellationToken)\n        {\n            /*\n             * Expected payload:\n             * {\n             *   \"commitSha\": \"abc123\",\n             *   \"committedAt\": \"2023-10-02T13:00:00Z\",\n             *   \"deployedAt\": \"2023-10-03T15:23:00Z\"\n             * }\n             */\n\n            var payload       = telemetryEvent.Payload;\n            var committedAt   = payload.GetProperty(\"committedAt\").GetDateTime();\n            var deployedAt    = payload.GetProperty(\"deployedAt\").GetDateTime();\n\n            var leadTimeHours = (deployedAt - committedAt).TotalHours;\n\n            var metricsDict = new Dictionary<string, object>\n            {\n                [\"LeadTimeHours\"] = leadTimeHours\n            };\n\n            var insight = new ProductivityInsight(\n                InsightType: \"deployment.lead-time\",\n                ObservedAtUtc: deployedAt,\n                Metrics: metricsDict);\n\n            return Task.FromResult<IReadOnlyCollection<ProductivityInsight>>(new[] { insight });\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Observer Pattern: Dispatcher --------------------------------------\n\n    public interface ITelemetryDispatcher\n    {\n        Task DispatchAsync(ProductivityInsight insight, CancellationToken token = default);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Emits insights to EventBridge, enabling downstream consumers (e.g. Glue, QuickSight)\n    /// to subscribe without tight coupling.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class EventBridgeDispatcher : ITelemetryDispatcher\n    {\n        private readonly IAmazonEventBridge _client;\n        private readonly string             _busName;\n        private readonly ILogger<EventBridgeDispatcher> _logger;\n\n        public EventBridgeDispatcher()\n            : this(new AmazonEventBridgeClient(),                                // Will auto-pick region/creds via Lambda environment.\n                   Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\"EVENT_BUS\") ?? \"pulseops-prod-bus\",\n                   LambdaLoggerWrapper.Create<EventBridgeDispatcher>())\n        { }\n\n        // DI constructor\n        public EventBridgeDispatcher(IAmazonEventBridge client, string busName, ILogger<EventBridgeDispatcher> logger)\n        {\n            _client  = client  ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(client));\n            _busName = busName ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(busName));\n            _logger  = logger  ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(logger));\n        }\n\n        public async Task DispatchAsync(ProductivityInsight insight, CancellationToken token = default)\n        {\n            var detailJson = JsonSerializer.Serialize(insight, JsonOptions.Options);\n\n            var request = new PutEventsRequest\n            {\n                Entries = new List<PutEventsRequestEntry>\n                {\n                    new()\n                    {\n                        EventBusName = _busName,\n                        Source       = \"pulseops.warehouse.transform\",\n                        DetailType   = insight.InsightType,\n                        Detail       = detailJson,\n                        Time         = DateTime.UtcNow\n                    }\n                }\n            };\n\n            var response = await _client.PutEventsAsync(request, token).ConfigureAwait(false);\n\n            if (response.FailedEntryCount > 0)\n            {\n                var errorEntry = response.Entries[0];\n                throw new InvalidOperationException(\n                    $\"EventBridge publish failed: {errorEntry.ErrorCode} \u2014 {errorEntry.ErrorMessage}\");\n            }\n\n            _logger.LogDebug(\"Insight '{InsightType}' dispatched to EventBridge bus '{BusName}'.\",\n                insight.InsightType, _busName);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n\n    #region Infrastructure -----------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Simple wrapper for Lambda's built-in ILogger.\n    /// </summary>\n    internal static class LambdaLoggerWrapper\n    {\n        public static ILogger<T> Create<T>() => new LambdaLogger<T>();\n\n        private sealed class LambdaLogger<T> : ILogger<T>, IDisposable\n        {\n            public void Log<TState>(LogLevel logLevel, EventId eventId, TState state, Exception? exception,\n                Func<TState, Exception?, string> formatter)\n            {\n                var message = formatter(state, exception);\n                var prefix  = $\"[{logLevel}] {typeof(T).Name}: \";\n\n                if (exception != null)\n                    LambdaLogger.Log($\"{prefix}{message}\\n{exception}\");\n                else\n                    LambdaLogger.Log($\"{prefix}{message}\");\n            }\n\n            public bool IsEnabled(LogLevel logLevel) => true;\n\n            public IDisposable BeginScope<TState>(TState state) => this;\n\n            public void Dispose() { } // No-op\n        }\n    }\n\n    internal static class JsonOptions\n    {\n        public static readonly JsonSerializerOptions Options = new()\n        {\n            PropertyNamingPolicy   = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,\n            WriteIndented          = false,\n            DefaultIgnoreCondition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull\n        };\n    }\n\n    internal sealed class SqsClientSingleton\n    {\n        public static readonly IAmazonSQS Instance =\n            new AmazonSQSClient(new EnvironmentVariablesAWSCredentials());\n    }\n\n    #endregion\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_68.txt",
    "numLines": 440,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 440
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_65.txt",
  "new_string": "    /// <summary>\n    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n    /// Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentBag to support parallel writes\n    /// from the EventTransformationPipeline's ActionBlock.\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n    {\n        private readonly ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n        public Task WriteAsync(\n            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n        {\n            foreach (var metric in metrics)\n            {\n                _buffer.Add(metric);\n            }\n            return Task.CompletedTask;\n        }\n\n        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n    }",
  "old_string": "    /// <summary>\n    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n    /// </summary>\n    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n    {\n        private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n        public Task WriteAsync(\n            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n        {\n            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n            return Task.CompletedTask;\n        }\n\n        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n    }",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
"Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it."

123. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Solution: csharp_data_warehouse_expert_012_bug_investigation_expert_01\n\n## Key Files Identified\n\n- **/app/project/src/module_65.txt** - Contains the `MemoryMetricSink` class with the race condition bug (Lines 305-322). This class uses a non-thread-safe `List<MetricDataPoint>` that is accessed concurrently from the `EventTransformationPipeline`.\n- **/app/project/src/module_65.txt** - Contains the `EventTransformationPipeline` class (Lines 328-461) that creates parallel workers with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism` setting, causing multiple threads to call `WriteAsync` on the sink concurrently.\n- **/app/project/src/module_23.txt** - The KPI transformation strategies module. Analysis confirmed deserialization logic is correct; the issue is upstream in the data flow.\n\n## Code Evidence\n\n### The Buggy Code - Non-Thread-Safe Buffer\n\n```csharp\n// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt\n// Lines: 305-322\n\n/// <summary>\n/// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n/// </summary>\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();  // BUG: Non-thread-safe List\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        _buffer.AddRange(metrics);  // RACE CONDITION: Multiple threads call this concurrently\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n### The Parallel Processing Context\n\n```csharp\n// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt\n// Lines: 384-408\n\n_sink = new ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>(\n    async (metric) =>\n    {\n        try\n        {\n            await metricSink.WriteAsync(\n                new[] { metric },\n                _cts.Token);\n        }\n        catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            _log.Error(\n                ex,\n                \"Failed to write metric {@Metric}\",\n                metric);\n            // In production, failed metrics would be forwarded to a DLQ.\n        }\n    },\n    new ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions\n    {\n        MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism,  // Default: 4 - causes concurrent access\n        BoundedCapacity        = boundedCapacity,\n        CancellationToken      = _cts.Token,\n        EnsureOrdered          = false\n    });\n```\n\n### The Pipeline Wiring (showing the connection)\n\n```csharp\n// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt\n// Lines: 467-491\n\npublic static class TransformationBootstrap\n{\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Configures Serilog and returns a ready-to-use pipeline for ad-hoc demos.\n    /// </summary>\n    public static EventTransformationPipeline CreateDemoPipeline()\n    {\n        // ... logging setup ...\n\n        var registry   = new StrategyRegistry(strategies);\n        var sink       = new MemoryMetricSink();  // The buggy sink is instantiated here\n        var pipeline   = new EventTransformationPipeline(registry, sink);  // And passed to pipeline\n\n        return pipeline;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Root Cause Identification\n\nThe root cause of the data corruption is a **race condition** in the `MemoryMetricSink` class within `/app/project/src/module_65.txt`. Specifically:\n\n1. **The `_buffer` field** (Line 310) is a `List<MetricDataPoint>`, which is **NOT thread-safe**.\n\n2. **The `WriteAsync` method** (Lines 312-318) calls `_buffer.AddRange(metrics)`, which modifies the internal array of the List.\n\n3. **The `EventTransformationPipeline`** class (Lines 328-461) uses TPL Dataflow's `ActionBlock` with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism` (default value: 4, as seen on Line 339).\n\n4. **When multiple threads** from the ActionBlock call `WriteAsync` concurrently, they simultaneously modify the non-thread-safe `List<T>`:\n   - Internal array resizing can cause data loss\n   - Concurrent writes to the same array indices cause data corruption\n   - The Count and internal array can become inconsistent\n\n### Why This Causes \"End of Stream\" Serialization Errors\n\nWhile the direct symptom mentioned in the task is `SerializationException: End of Stream encountered before parsing was completed`, this race condition in `MemoryMetricSink` can cause:\n\n1. **Corrupted or truncated data** being written to the buffer\n2. **Missing data** when concurrent resize operations discard partially written elements\n3. **Jumbled byte sequences** when array boundaries become inconsistent\n\nWhen downstream components attempt to deserialize data that was corrupted during the parallel write operations, they encounter incomplete or malformed byte arrays, resulting in the serialization exception.\n\n### Architectural Pattern Identified\n\nThe codebase correctly uses the **Strategy Pattern** for KPI transformations and the **Pipeline Pattern** (via TPL Dataflow) for high-throughput processing. However, the `MemoryMetricSink` violates the thread-safety contract expected by the pipeline's parallel execution model.\n\n## Implementation (Proposed Fix)\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n- `/app/project/src/module_65.txt`\n\n### Changes Required\n\nReplace the non-thread-safe `List<MetricDataPoint>` with a thread-safe `ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint>`:\n\n**Before (Buggy Code):**\n```csharp\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n**After (Fixed Code):**\n```csharp\n/// <summary>\n/// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n/// Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentBag to support parallel writes\n/// from the EventTransformationPipeline's ActionBlock.\n/// </summary>\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        foreach (var metric in metrics)\n        {\n            _buffer.Add(metric);\n        }\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n### Alternative Fix (Using Lock)\n\nIf ordering matters or if `ConcurrentBag` is not preferred, a `lock` statement can be used:\n\n```csharp\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n    private readonly object _lock = new();\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        lock (_lock)\n        {\n            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n        }\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n    {\n        lock (_lock)\n        {\n            return _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Testing the Fix\n\nThe fix can be verified by:\n1. Running the existing parallel processing test in `/app/project/tests/test_main.txt` (Line 115-158: `Engine_Processes_Messages_In_Parallel`)\n2. Creating a stress test that submits thousands of events concurrently to the pipeline\n3. Verifying no exceptions occur and all data is correctly captured in the sink\n\n## Summary\n\n- **Primary finding**: Race condition in `MemoryMetricSink` class due to non-thread-safe `List<T>` being accessed concurrently from `EventTransformationPipeline`'s parallel ActionBlock workers.\n\n- **Key components**:\n  - `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` - `MemoryMetricSink` (buggy sink, Lines 305-322)\n  - `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` - `EventTransformationPipeline` (parallel orchestrator, Lines 328-461)\n  - `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` - `TransformationBootstrap` (wiring, Lines 467-491)\n\n- **Architectural pattern**: Strategy Pattern + Pipeline Pattern (TPL Dataflow) with thread-safety violation in sink component\n\n- **Root cause**: Multiple threads from `ActionBlock` with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism > 1` concurrently calling `List<T>.AddRange()`, which is not thread-safe\n\n- **Recommended fix**: Replace `List<MetricDataPoint>` with `ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint>` (or use explicit `lock` synchronization)\n\n- **Why the bug is intermittent**: The race condition only manifests under high load when multiple parallel workers happen to write to the buffer simultaneously, causing internal array corruption that leads to downstream serialization failures\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Solution: csharp_data_warehouse_expert_012_bug_investigation_expert_01\n\n## Key Files Identified\n\n- **/app/project/src/module_65.txt** - Contains the `MemoryMetricSink` class with the race condition bug (Lines 305-322). This class uses a non-thread-safe `List<MetricDataPoint>` that is accessed concurrently from the `EventTransformationPipeline`.\n- **/app/project/src/module_65.txt** - Contains the `EventTransformationPipeline` class (Lines 328-461) that creates parallel workers with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism` setting, causing multiple threads to call `WriteAsync` on the sink concurrently.\n- **/app/project/src/module_23.txt** - The KPI transformation strategies module. Analysis confirmed deserialization logic is correct; the issue is upstream in the data flow.\n\n## Code Evidence\n\n### The Buggy Code - Non-Thread-Safe Buffer\n\n```csharp\n// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt\n// Lines: 305-322\n\n/// <summary>\n/// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n/// </summary>\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();  // BUG: Non-thread-safe List\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        _buffer.AddRange(metrics);  // RACE CONDITION: Multiple threads call this concurrently\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n### The Parallel Processing Context\n\n```csharp\n// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt\n// Lines: 384-408\n\n_sink = new ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>(\n    async (metric) =>\n    {\n        try\n        {\n            await metricSink.WriteAsync(\n                new[] { metric },\n                _cts.Token);\n        }\n        catch (Exception ex)\n        {\n            _log.Error(\n                ex,\n                \"Failed to write metric {@Metric}\",\n                metric);\n            // In production, failed metrics would be forwarded to a DLQ.\n        }\n    },\n    new ExecutionDataflowBlockOptions\n    {\n        MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism,  // Default: 4 - causes concurrent access\n        BoundedCapacity        = boundedCapacity,\n        CancellationToken      = _cts.Token,\n        EnsureOrdered          = false\n    });\n```\n\n### The Pipeline Wiring (showing the connection)\n\n```csharp\n// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt\n// Lines: 467-491\n\npublic static class TransformationBootstrap\n{\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Configures Serilog and returns a ready-to-use pipeline for ad-hoc demos.\n    /// </summary>\n    public static EventTransformationPipeline CreateDemoPipeline()\n    {\n        // ... logging setup ...\n\n        var registry   = new StrategyRegistry(strategies);\n        var sink       = new MemoryMetricSink();  // The buggy sink is instantiated here\n        var pipeline   = new EventTransformationPipeline(registry, sink);  // And passed to pipeline\n\n        return pipeline;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Root Cause Identification\n\nThe root cause of the data corruption is a **race condition** in the `MemoryMetricSink` class within `/app/project/src/module_65.txt`. Specifically:\n\n1. **The `_buffer` field** (Line 310) is a `List<MetricDataPoint>`, which is **NOT thread-safe**.\n\n2. **The `WriteAsync` method** (Lines 312-318) calls `_buffer.AddRange(metrics)`, which modifies the internal array of the List.\n\n3. **The `EventTransformationPipeline`** class (Lines 328-461) uses TPL Dataflow's `ActionBlock` with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism = maxDegreeOfParallelism` (default value: 4, as seen on Line 339).\n\n4. **When multiple threads** from the ActionBlock call `WriteAsync` concurrently, they simultaneously modify the non-thread-safe `List<T>`:\n   - Internal array resizing can cause data loss\n   - Concurrent writes to the same array indices cause data corruption\n   - The Count and internal array can become inconsistent\n\n### Why This Causes \"End of Stream\" Serialization Errors\n\nWhile the direct symptom mentioned in the task is `SerializationException: End of Stream encountered before parsing was completed`, this race condition in `MemoryMetricSink` can cause:\n\n1. **Corrupted or truncated data** being written to the buffer\n2. **Missing data** when concurrent resize operations discard partially written elements\n3. **Jumbled byte sequences** when array boundaries become inconsistent\n\nWhen downstream components attempt to deserialize data that was corrupted during the parallel write operations, they encounter incomplete or malformed byte arrays, resulting in the serialization exception.\n\n### Architectural Pattern Identified\n\nThe codebase correctly uses the **Strategy Pattern** for KPI transformations and the **Pipeline Pattern** (via TPL Dataflow) for high-throughput processing. However, the `MemoryMetricSink` violates the thread-safety contract expected by the pipeline's parallel execution model.\n\n## Implementation (Proposed Fix)\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n- `/app/project/src/module_65.txt`\n\n### Changes Required\n\nReplace the non-thread-safe `List<MetricDataPoint>` with a thread-safe `ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint>`:\n\n**Before (Buggy Code):**\n```csharp\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n**After (Fixed Code):**\n```csharp\n/// <summary>\n/// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).\n/// Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentBag to support parallel writes\n/// from the EventTransformationPipeline's ActionBlock.\n/// </summary>\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        foreach (var metric in metrics)\n        {\n            _buffer.Add(metric);\n        }\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n}\n```\n\n### Alternative Fix (Using Lock)\n\nIf ordering matters or if `ConcurrentBag` is not preferred, a `lock` statement can be used:\n\n```csharp\npublic sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink\n{\n    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();\n    private readonly object _lock = new();\n\n    public Task WriteAsync(\n        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,\n        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)\n    {\n        lock (_lock)\n        {\n            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);\n        }\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()\n    {\n        lock (_lock)\n        {\n            return _buffer.ToImmutableArray();\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Testing the Fix\n\nThe fix can be verified by:\n1. Running the existing parallel processing test in `/app/project/tests/test_main.txt` (Line 115-158: `Engine_Processes_Messages_In_Parallel`)\n2. Creating a stress test that submits thousands of events concurrently to the pipeline\n3. Verifying no exceptions occur and all data is correctly captured in the sink\n\n## Summary\n\n- **Primary finding**: Race condition in `MemoryMetricSink` class due to non-thread-safe `List<T>` being accessed concurrently from `EventTransformationPipeline`'s parallel ActionBlock workers.\n\n- **Key components**:\n  - `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` - `MemoryMetricSink` (buggy sink, Lines 305-322)\n  - `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` - `EventTransformationPipeline` (parallel orchestrator, Lines 328-461)\n  - `/app/project/src/module_65.txt` - `TransformationBootstrap` (wiring, Lines 467-491)\n\n- **Architectural pattern**: Strategy Pattern + Pipeline Pattern (TPL Dataflow) with thread-safety violation in sink component\n\n- **Root cause**: Multiple threads from `ActionBlock` with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism > 1` concurrently calling `List<T>.AddRange()`, which is not thread-safe\n\n- **Recommended fix**: Replace `List<MetricDataPoint>` with `ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint>` (or use explicit `lock` synchronization)\n\n- **Why the bug is intermittent**: The race condition only manifests under high load when multiple parallel workers happen to write to the buffer simultaneously, causing internal array corruption that leads to downstream serialization failures\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/solution.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

        Code Changes (3)
        
1. EDIT /app/project/src/module_65.txt
Before
    /// <summary>
    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink
    {
        private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();

        public Task WriteAsync(
            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,
            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)
        {
            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()
            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();
    }
After
    /// <summary>
    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).
    /// Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentBag to support parallel writes
    /// from the EventTransformationPipeline's ActionBlock.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink
    {
        private readonly ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();

        public Task WriteAsync(
            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,
            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)
        {
            foreach (var metric in metrics)
            {
                _buffer.Add(metric);
            }
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()
            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();
    }
2. EDIT /app/project/src/module_65.txt
Before
    /// <summary>
    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).
    /// </summary>

    {

        public Task WriteAsync(
            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,
            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)
        {
            _buffer.AddRange(metrics);
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()
            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();
    }
After
    /// <summary>
    /// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).
    /// Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentBag to support parallel writes
    /// from the EventTransformationPipeline's ActionBlock.
    /// </summary>
    public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink
    {
        private readonly ConcurrentBag<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();

        public Task WriteAsync(
            IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,
            CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)
        {
            foreach (var metric in metrics)
            {
                _buffer.Add(metric);
            }
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()
            => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();
    }
3. WRITE /logs/agent/solution.md
# Solution: csharp_data_warehouse_expert_012_bug_investigation_expert_01

## Key Files Identified

- **/app/project/src/module_65.txt** - Contains the `MemoryMetricSink` class with the race condition bug (Lines 305-322). This class uses a non-thread-safe `List<MetricDataPoint>` that is accessed concurrently from the `EventTransformationPipeline`.
- **/app/project/src/module_65.txt** - Contains the `EventTransformationPipeline` class (Lines 328-461) that creates parallel workers with `MaxDegreeOfParallelism` setting, causing multiple threads to call `WriteAsync` on the sink concurrently.
- **/app/project/src/module_23.txt** - The KPI transformation strategies module. Analysis confirmed deserialization logic is correct; the issue is upstream in the data flow.

## Code Evidence

### The Buggy Code - Non-Thread-Safe Buffer

```csharp
// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt
// Lines: 305-322

/// <summary>
/// In-memory metric sink (primarily for unit tests and local debugging).
/// </summary>
public sealed class MemoryMetricSink : IMetricSink
{
    private readonly List<MetricDataPoint> _buffer = new();  // BUG: Non-thread-safe List

    public Task WriteAsync(
        IReadOnlyCollection<MetricDataPoint> metrics,
        CancellationToken                     cancellationToken = default)
    {
        _buffer.AddRange(metrics);  // RACE CONDITION: Multiple threads call this concurrently
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }

    public IReadOnlyList<MetricDataPoint> Snapshot()
        => _buffer.ToImmutableArray();
}
```

### The Parallel Processing Context

```csharp
// File: /app/project/src/module_65.txt
// Lines: 384-408

_sink = new ActionBlock<MetricDataPoint>(
    async (metric) =>
    {
        try
        {
            await metricSink.WriteAsync(
              
... (truncated)

6. $ grep -r "new Queue<MemoryStream>\|Queue<Stream>\|Queue<byte\[\]>" /app/project --include="*.txt" 2>/dev/null

10. $ grep -rn "Queue<" /app/project/src --include="*.txt" | grep -v "Concurrent"

15. $ grep -C 10 "GetStream\|ReturnStream\|Acquire.*Stream\|Release.*Stream" /app/project/src/module_64.txt
16. $ grep -C 10 "GetStream\|ReturnStream\|Acquire.*Stream\|Release.*Stream" /app/project/src/module_60.txt
